name: Aino

builds.weapons:
1. Favonius Greatsword (4✩)*
2. Makhaira Aquamarine (4✩)*
3. Flame-Forged Insight [5] (4✩)*
≈ Master Key (4✩)*
builds.artifacts: Conditional (See Notes)
builds.main_stats:
Sands - Energy Recharge / Elemental Mastery
Goblet - Elemental Mastery
Circlet - Elemental Mastery / Crit Rate
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge
2. Elemental Mastery
3. Crit Rate*
builds.tips:
Aino can offer Off-Field Hydro application with her Burst. She does not possess a Moon Wheel, and cannot contribute to Lunar-Reactions as the sole member in the team from Nod-Krai. Aino's scalings are pitifully low, thus, her damage investment is not a priority.

Aino's [C4] gives her 10 Energy upon landing her Skill on an enemy, effectively changing her Burst cost from 50 to 40. This has a cooldown of 10 seconds.

Aino's A1 talent allows her Burst to attack more frequently and have a larger AoE DMG if Moonsign: Ascendant Gleam is active. This requires 2 or more Nod-Krai characters in the party. Aino herself counts towards this.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Greatsword: favo.
Makhaira Aquamarine: If the team does not benefit from the ATK% buff, this weapon is not worth using.
Flame-Forged Insight: If Aino cannot trigger the specified Elemental Reactions, this weapon is not worth using. This weapon does not need to be leveled if you do not care about Aino's personal damage.
Master Key: Refinements for this weapon are not needed if you do not care about Aino's personal damage.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

name: Albedo

2. Uraku Misugiri (5✩)

builds.artifacts:
1. Husk of Opulent Dreams (4)*
≈ Golden Troupe (4)
2. Husk of Opulent Dreams (2) / Archaic Petra (2) / Golden Troupe (2) [Choose Two]
3. Archaic Petra (4)*
4. Tenacity of the Millelith (4)*
builds.main_stats:
Sands - DEF%
Goblet - DEF% / Geo DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. DEF%
3. Energy Recharge
4. ATK%
builds.tips:
Albedo is a character that can summon a Geo Construct from his Skill which deals damage on a 2 second interval whenever enemies in the field are damaged. His Burst provides 125 Elemental Mastery to the party, which is good on teams that rely on amplification reactions, such as Vaporize. It is worth noting that since his stat priority is focused on DEF (Skill scaling), you might want to skip casting Burst if your team doesn't benefit from the 125 Elemental Mastery buff.

Casting Albedo's Skill on top of a boss will summon a Silver Isotoma as long as the initial cast hits the opponent.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Peak Patrol Song: Make sure you Normal Attack twice before or after using his Skill with this weapon.
Harbinger of Dawn: Assumes the passive is active. Without the passive active most of the time, this weapon is not worth using.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Husk of Opulent Dreams: Due to Albedo's Skill snapshotting upon cast, you need to either wait ~12 seconds before starting a domain/fight to reach max stacks or recast his Skill to benefit from the DEF and Geo DMG bonus provided by the 4 piece set effect.
Archaic Petra: This set is a niche option for Albedo. It is lower personal damage than several other set options, but can be a reasonable option in some teams for buffing other characters. Do note that the 4pc bonus requires you to pick the shard up on Albedo.
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set is used for the 4 piece set effect which has good uptime on Albedo. Worth noting that this set decreases your Skill damage by a lot if compared to the other offensive sets.

builds.weapons:

Conditional (See Notes)

builds.main_stats:
Sands - Elemental Mastery / ATK%
Goblet - Dendro DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. Crit Rate / DMG
3. Elemental Mastery*
4. ATK%
builds.tips:
This playstyle maximizes Alhaitham's kit, leveraging both Spread reactions and his Elemental Mastery-scaling Skill for significant damage. As a Spread DPS with a high rate of on-field Dendro application and flexible team compositions, he excels in Quicken/Quickbloom teams. To further maximise his performance it's highly recommended to prioritize levelling him to 90/90.

Basic Rotations:

- Burst up = Q > E > N2D + 2N3D + N1C + 2N2C
- Burst down = E > N1C + 5N2C + N1

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Light of Foliar Incision: This weapon prefers Elemental Mastery sands even in situations with fewer or no Spread procs such as in Bloom/Burgeon/Hyperbloom teams.
Uraku Misugiri: With a Geo unit on the team this performs on par with Light of Foliar Incision, or even slightly better in AoE scenarios where you lose stacks faster on Light of Foliar Incision.
Wolf-Fang: At [R5], this weapon performs similarly to Haran Geppaku Futsu and Mistsplitter Reforged.
Toukabou Shigure: This ranking assumes single-target situations, in AoE situations it starts falling behind weapons such as Iron Sting and Blackcliff Longsword.
Harbinger of Dawn: If you can maintain near perfect uptime on its passive this weapon will perform similarly to weapons like Haran Geppaku Futsu.
Xiphos' Moonlight: This weapon can perform similarly to The Black Sword in teams where you have higher Energy Recharge requirements or want to Burst every rotation.
Festering Desire: This weapon can perform similarly to The Black Sword in teams where you have higher Energy Recharge requirements or want to Burst every rotation.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Gilded Dreams: This ranking assumes that you already have another Deepwood Memories holder on the team, if not then Deepwood Memories becomes his best set.
+80 EM set: These split set options can actually perform similarly to Gilded Dreams (4) or even surpass it in situations where you have higher Energy Recharge requirements or want to Burst every rotation. Or even if they just have better substat rolls on them.
Marechaussee Hunter: If played with Furina, this is Alhaitham's best set.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: Certain Quicken teams can run little to no Energy Recharge and simply Burst every other rotation without a dps loss
Elemental Mastery: If you're running under 200 Elemental Mastery this substat has priority over Crit

name: Aloy

builds.weapons:
1. Thundering Pulse (5✩)
2. Polar Star (5✩)*
3. Skyward Harp (5✩)
4. The Stringless (4✩)*
5. Amos' Bow (5✩)
6. Alley Hunter (4✩)

1. Blizzard Strayer (4)*
2. Noblesse Oblige (2) / Blizzard Strayer (2)
3. Emblem of Severed Fate (4)
4. Noblesse Oblige (2) / Blizzard Strayer (2) / +80 EM set (2) / +18% ATK set (2) / +20% ER set (2) [Choose Two]*
5. Noblesse Oblige (4)*
≈ Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4)
builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK% / Elemental Mastery
Goblet - Cryo DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. ATK%
3. Elemental Mastery*
4. Energy Recharge
builds.tips:
Aloy is much preferred as a Burst Support over Main DPS as getting coil stacks for her Cryo infusion can be quite unreliable. Aloy needs 4 coil stacks to get the Cryo infusion and you can only get 1 stack every 0.1 seconds which is quite hard to do considering the Bomblets are scattered all over the place. One thing to note regarding Aloy's coil stacks is that she can still get coil stacks even when she's off-field.

Aloy is very niche as she only performs well in certain comps and even then, she's still completely replaceable by other characters. She can be utilized on a Freeze team where she can be a battery and nuke at the same time since she provides 5 Cryo particles on her Skill. She can also be utilized on a Melt team where you can Melt her Burst for quite a decent amount of damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Polar Star: Forcing to get Polar Star stacks isn't worth due to consuming extra time to get those stacks.
The Stringless: Placement assumes Aloy is used on a Reverse Melt team. On Reverse Melt Aloy The Stringless, especially at higher refinement levels, can pull ahead of Thundering Pulse.
Prototype Crescent: Assumes the passive is active by proccing a weak point shot. This weapon especially at higher refinement levels can be as good as Skyward Harp.
Sacrificial Bow: A pretty decent option to make Aloy a Cryo battery as her Skill provides 5 Cryo particles.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Blizzard Strayer: This artifact set is best used on a Freeze-focused Aloy as it provides a ton of Crit Value.
+80 EM set: This artifact set bonus only benefits a Melt-focused Aloy, Elemental Mastery provides nothing on a Freeze Aloy.
Noblesse Oblige: Aloy is one of the best Noblesse Oblige (4) holders since she has low energy requirements and cooldown for her Burst.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Elemental Mastery: Only an option if your Aloy is played on a Reverse Melt team as it provides nothing to a Freeze Aloy.

builds.tips:
Arlecchino is a Pyro DPS that focuses on dealing damage through her Bond of Life mechanic. It should be noted that while in combat, Arlecchino cannot be healed by any source other than her Burst. For this reason, a shielder is strongly recommended within her teams.

Combo: E > Team buffs > CA > 3(N5D) or 6(N3D). 6(N3D) is preferred for Vaporize and Melt teams.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Staff of the Scarlet Sands: If Arlecchino is not triggering Vaporize or Melt, do not use an Elemental Mastery Sands with this weapon and it ranks lower than Staff of Homa.
Vortex Vanquisher: Ranking assumes you are shielded. Without a shield, this weapon performs similarly to White Tassel [R5].
Deathmatch: At higher refinements, this weapon will perform similarly to Primordial Jade Winged-Spear.
Ballad of the Fjords: If Arlecchino is not triggering Vaporize or Melt, do not use this weapon. At higher refinements, this weapon will perform similarly to Primordial Jade Winged-Spear.
Missive Windspear: If Arlecchino is not triggering Vaporize or Melt, do not use this weapon.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Desert Pavilion Chronicle: Don't cancel Arlecchino's Charged Attack too early to ensure that it hits opponents for the passive effect.
Echoes of an Offering: This set performs similarly to Desert Pavilion Chronicle (4) if your ping is consistently lower than 200.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Elemental Mastery: Should only be considered if Arlecchino is in a Vaporize or Melt team.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK%
Goblet - Cryo DMG
Circlet - Crit DMG / ATK%
builds.substats:
1. Crit DMG
2. ATK%
3. Energy Recharge
4. Crit Rate*
builds.tips:
Kamisato Ayaka is a pretty straightforward DPS that deals most of her damage from her Burst. She has very high scalings on her Burst at the cost of it being 80 energy. You will likely need another Cryo battery on the team to provide energy for her (Diona, Kaeya, Rosaria, etc). She has a special dash (like Mona) and after she dashes, she will get a Cryo infusion on her Normal Attacks. Ensure you always have this bonus active to maximise your rotation damage.

Kamisato Ayaka's Charged Attack, Skill and Burst is able to hit enemies sucked inside Venti's Burst.

Her Burst also stays in place on frozen enemies and slightly knocks enemies away when they're not frozen.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Primordial Jade Cutter: With Blizzard Strayer (4) and Cryo Resonance, this weapon overcrits, so try to avoid getting Crit Rate substats.
Finale of the Deep: Weapon rankings are assuming you clear the Bond of Life. If you cannot clear the Bond of Life, it performs similarly to Amenoma Kageuchi.
Aquila Favonia: While it has Physical DMG as a main stat, which is basically useless on Kamisato Ayaka, it is still a good statstick as it has a high base ATK and ATK% buff from the passive, however, it's not a huge upgrade over the other 4 star options and there might be a character which can utilize this weapon better (such as Bennett).
Skyward Blade: The Energy Recharge main stat provided from this sword makes Kamisato Ayaka comfortable in terms of having a good Burst uptime, try to avoid getting Energy Recharge substats since the weapon already provides you with enough Energy Recharge.
Amenoma Kageuchi: Lowers Kamisato Ayaka's Energy Recharge requirement which allows her to allocate some of her Energy Recharge substats to other offensive substats.
Harbinger of Dawn: A good Crit statstick if you can keep the requirements up (having >90% Flat HP).

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: Loses value here assuming you're running Blizzard Strayer (4), if you're not using that artifact set, then it has the same priority as Crit DMG (You still want to aim for 1:2 ratio in the end)

notes:
Kamisato Ayaka is a pretty straightforward DPS that deals most of her damage from her Burst. She has very high scalings on her Burst at the cost of it being 80 energy. You will likely need another Cryo battery on the team to provide energy for her (Diona, Kaeya, Rosaria, etc). She has a special dash (like Mona) and after she dashes, she will get a Cryo infusion on her Normal Attacks. Ensure you always have this bonus active to maximise your rotation damage.

Kamisato Ayaka's Charged Attack, Skill and Burst is able to hit enemies sucked inside Venti's Burst.

Her Burst also stays in place on frozen enemies and slightly knocks enemies away when they're not frozen.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Primordial Jade Cutter: With Blizzard Strayer (4) and Cryo Resonance, this weapon overcrits, so try to avoid getting Crit Rate substats.
Finale of the Deep: Weapon rankings are assuming you clear the Bond of Life. If you cannot clear the Bond of Life, it performs similarly to Amenoma Kageuchi.
Aquila Favonia: While it has Physical DMG as a main stat, which is basically useless on Kamisato Ayaka, it is still a good statstick as it has a high base ATK and ATK% buff from the passive, however, it's not a huge upgrade over the other 4 star options and there might be a character which can utilize this weapon better (such as Bennett).
Skyward Blade: The Energy Recharge main stat provided from this sword makes Kamisato Ayaka comfortable in terms of having a good Burst uptime, try to avoid getting Energy Recharge substats since the weapon already provides you with enough Energy Recharge.
Amenoma Kageuchi: Lowers Kamisato Ayaka's Energy Recharge requirement which allows her to allocate some of her Energy Recharge substats to other offensive substats.
Harbinger of Dawn: A good Crit statstick if you can keep the requirements up (having >90% Flat HP).

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: Loses value here assuming you're running Blizzard Strayer (4), if you're not using that artifact set, then it has the same priority as Crit DMG (You still want to aim for 1:2 ratio in the end)

builds.tips:
DPS Kamisato Ayato focuses on doing large amounts of on-field damage with his Skill and Burst.

Try to activate Kamisato Ayato's Burst before using his Skill to maximize his Normal Attack DMG. Getting enough Energy Recharge for this is important, and varies based on team. Since the casting time is so long, it may not be worth snapshotting into buffs.

The amount of slashes Kamisato Ayato can do during an Skill window is sometimes influenced by the player's set FPS.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Skyward Blade: This ranking assumes Kamisato Ayato needs 135% Energy Recharge to use his Burst every rotation. Its ranking changes based on his Energy Recharge Requirements.
Summit Shaper: In teams where ATK% buffs are scarce, this weapon has the potential to rival The Black Sword.
Finale of the Deep: If you can reliably clear the Bond of Life, this weapon is better than the weapons ranked equal to it.
The Black Sword: At [R5] this weapon is roughly equal to Summit Shaper.
Lion's Roar: In Electro-Charged teams, this weapon performs about equal to Summit Shaper.
Harbinger of Dawn: Only recommended with a shielder. If shielded, this weapon is better than Finale of the Deep and can rival Splendor of Tranquil Waters in teams with high amounts of ATK% buffs.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Nymph's Dream: Reliant on Kamisato Ayato's Water Illusion hitting enemies which can be inconsistent against some content. Without the third stack of the passive, this set becomes about equal to +18% ATK set (2) +15% Hydro DMG Bonus set (2).
Blizzard Strayer: When run in a Freeze Team, this is Kamisato Ayato's best set. Note that it is not recommended to farm specifically for this set.
Thundering Fury: When run in an Electro-Charged team, this is Kamisato Ayato's best set due to the Cooldown Reduction on Skill allowing for shorter rotations. Note that it is not recommended to farm specifically for this set.
Echoes of an Offering: The performance of this artifact set is inconsistent, and is based on user ping and other factors. It is not recommended to run this set unless your ping is consistently below 100. If you can achieve this, Echoes of an Offering (4) is competitive with Heart of Depth (4).
Marechaussee Hunter: If Kamisato Ayato is used in a Burgeon team where he consistently takes damage, or with Furina, this is his best set.

2. Ocean-Hued Clam (4)

builds.main_stats:
Sands - HP% / Energy Recharge
Goblet - HP%
Circlet - HP% / Healing Bonus
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. HP%
3. Flat HP
4. Crit Rate*
builds.tips:
Baizhu provides teamwide healing through the use of his Skill, and heals the active character from his Burst. The shields generated from his Burst should be considered as interrupt resistance as opposed to a reliable form of damage mitigation for non-Bloom reaction related damage sources. Due to being a Dendro catalyst, Baizhu can work effectively in a large range of different Dendro team archetypes.

Baizhu's Skill has a 2-3 second delay between cast time and heal received, keep this in mind when swapping to low HP allies after Baizhu's rotation.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Prototype Amber: In addition to the amount of flat energy generation for the user, this weapon can provide significant team-wide healing when used in conjunction with Healing Bonus. It is a great option when using Baizhu with Furina for building Fanfare points from the second rotation onwards.
Favonius Codex: favo.
Hakushin Ring: This weapon is best used in Quicken teams when Baizhu is able to provide reasonable uptime on the buff.
A Thousand Floating Dreams: This weapon can be considered in certain teams for the modest Elemental Mastery team buff it provides, though there are generally better uses for it.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Deepwood Memories: Under AoE situations, it may be better for another party member to hold this set as Baizhu has lackluster AoE.
Instructor: The Elemental Mastery gained from this set outweighs the reduction in A4 passive bonus from lower main stat values.
Noblesse Oblige: In teams that benefit from ATK%, this set can be run over Instructor (4) if the additional sustain from higher HP values is preferred.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - HP%
Goblet - HP%
Circlet - Healing Bonus
builds.substats:
1. HP%
2. Flat HP
builds.tips:
Barbara's main role is to provide sustain via her Skill. Due to the lack of particle generation, building Energy Recharge to Burst every rotation is not feasible. Her Burst should be treated as an emergency button.

Barbara's Skill gives you a pulse of healing every 5 seconds, every pulse will imbue you with a Hydro status, that means it can be used for status-cleansing (Pyro in specific, while other elements may take more pulses).

Take note that Hydro reacting with Cryo status will freeze you in place, her Skill should be used in consideration of the environment.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Everlasting Moonglow: This weapon focuses on maximizing Barbara's personal damage when played on field. However, Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers commonly results in the highest team damage if someone can make use of the buff.
Hakushin Ring: This weapon is a strong pick for teams that make good use of its passive and let Barbara trigger Electro reactions reliably.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Ocean-Hued Clam: This set is the best generalist pick as it lets Barbara deal decent damage of her own while healing.
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: If Barbara can reliably trigger reactions, this set can be the best option due to the buffs it can provide.
Instructor: If Barbara can reliably trigger reactions, this set can be the best option due to the buffs it can provide.

builds.artifacts:
1. Noblesse Oblige (4)
2. Instructor (4)*
3. Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4)*
4. Deepwood Memories (4)*
builds.main_stats:
Sands - Energy Recharge / ATK% / HP%
Goblet - Pyro DMG / HP%
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG / HP% / Healing Bonus
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge
2. Crit Rate / DMG / HP%
3. ATK% / Flat HP
builds.tips:
Bennett's main utility as a support comes from the huge Flat ATK buff his Burst provides. This buff only scales with his Base Attack, so it is unaffected by any external ATK buff.

Bennett's C6 infuses melee weapons with Pyro, which can mess up a few teams. However, if you have C6 Bennett, you more than likely have some of the supports those teams prefer over Bennett anyway.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Mistsplitter Reforged: While providing the same Base Attack as Aquila Favonia, this weapon is a better choice when building for Bennett's personal damage.
Absolution: While providing the same Base Attack as Aquila Favonia, this weapon is a better choice when building for Bennett's personal damage.
Azurelight: While providing the same Base Attack as Aquila Favonia, this weapon is a better choice when building for Bennett's personal damage.
Freedom-Sworn: In teams where Bennett can reliably trigger this weapon's passive, it can be stronger than Aquila Favonia, Mistsplitter Reforged, and Absolution.
Peak Patrol Song: While requiring two additional Normal Attacks, this weapon can provide a sizeable buff with just one or two DEF% main stats, making it competitive with Aquila Favonia and similar options for teams that highly value DMG Bonus buffs.
Favonius Sword: This weapon's low Base Attack heavily impairs Bennett's buffing potency, but it can be a viable pick in teams with extremely high Energy Recharge requirements or no actual use for the Flat ATK buff.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Instructor: In teams that value Elemental Mastery more than ATK%, this set pulls ahead of other options if Bennett can reliably trigger a reaction.
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: This set requires Bennett to trigger a reaction including the element you want to buff. When Noblesse Oblige is already covered on the team, or the team values DMG Bonus more than ATK% or Elemental Mastery, this set is a good pick.
Deepwood Memories: The best pick for any Dendro damage related team Bennett might be in. Use this if there is no other viable holder available.

Deepwood Memories: The best pick for any Dendro damage related team Bennett might be in. Use this if there is no other viable holder available.

name: Candace

builds.weapons:

builds.artifacts:

2. Emblem of Severed Fate (4)*
3. Instructor (4)
4. +20% ER set (2) / +15% Hydro DMG Bonus set (2) / Noblesse Oblige (2) / +20% HP set (2) [Choose Two]
builds.main_stats:
Sands - Energy Recharge / HP%
Goblet - HP% / Hydro DMG
Circlet - HP% / Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge
2. Crit Rate*
3. HP%
4. Flat HP
builds.tips:
Candace is a 4-star Hydro Support who specializes in providing off-field Hydro application with her HP-scaling Burst, infusing melee allies' Normal Attacks with Hydro and providing defensive utility with her Skill.

It is worth noting that Candace will never be the strongest option for your teams. At the cost of having different kinds of utility in one kit, she will never be the best at them. Her damage contribution is surpassed by offensive Hydro supports like Xingqiu, and her defensive capabilities are outclassed by other Hydro healers.

Because of this, it is recommended to focus on Candace's strength as a consistent Hydro applier by getting as much Energy Recharge as possible, helping her maintain consistent Burst uptime. Her weapon and artifact rankings therefore focus on Particle/Energy generation and Recharge.

In some teams, Candace's hold Skill cooldown is too long for rotations to work properly. If Candace is not C4, avoid getting hit and use a tap Skill as quickly as possible.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: This is Candace's best option because of her drastically high Energy Recharge requirement. If she is the only Hydro character on the team, she will need nearly 300% Energy Recharge to maintain consistent Burst uptime. With another Hydro character, this requirement drops to around 200-250% Energy Recharge. This kind of requirement is difficult to reach with most weapons, but each Favonius Lance passive proc cuts this requirement down by about 20%.
Staff of Homa: Can be considered if you want Candace to have high personal damage, which is usually notable at C6.
Deathmatch: Can be considered if you want Candace to have high personal damage, which is usually notable at C6.
"The Catch": Candace's strongest F2P option thanks to its high Energy Recharge and Burst-enhancing passive.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: Even though Candace is not from Natlan, she can still take advantage of the 12% DMG Bonus buff for the team, alongside the Flat Energy refund from Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (2) if there is a Nightsoul Burst being activated on the team.
Emblem of Severed Fate: The only necessary artifact stat for Candace is the 20% Energy Recharge from the 2-piece effect. The 4-piece effect lets her better capitalize on the high Energy Recharge amounts she wants to equip, making it her strongest personal option. However, Emblem of Severed Fate is a highly contested artifact set and thus it is possible for you to end up using the 2-piece + 2-piece alternatives listed above. 

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

builds.tips:
Charlotte supports the team through her healing, and minor off-field damage. Though she has ATK% scaling on her healing, her personal damage is rather poor and support based artifacts and weapons are recommended.

Charlotte's Hold Skill will change to Photo Mode if the Special Analysis Zoom Lens gadget is toggled on. Be sure to toggle it off in the inventory if you wish to properly use her Hold Skill in combat.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Codex: favo.
Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers: Due to Charlotte's high Energy Recharge needs, this weapon may not be suitable to run in some teams.
Wine and Song: It is very important to sprint before casting Charlotte's Burst so that she gains the extra ATK% for her Burst.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Blizzard Strayer: The main utility for this set is more consistent Favonius Codex triggers. However, it also slightly helps her damage, which can be valuable at higher Constellations.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
Crit Rate: Only more useful than Crit DMG when using Favonius Codex.

≈ The First Great Magic (5✩)
3. Thundering Pulse (5✩)

5. Amos' Bow (5✩)*
≈ Polar Star (5✩)*
≈ Scion of the Blazing Sun (4✩)*
6. Chain Breaker [5] (4✩)

Marechaussee Hunter (4)*
builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK%
Goblet - ATK%
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. ATK%
3. Elemental Mastery
4. Energy Recharge*
builds.tips:
Chasca is a DPS that focuses on dealing damage through her converted Shining Shadowhunt Shells. Her Anemo damage is considerably low, therefore she is best played with multiple different PHEC (Pyro/Hydro/Electro/Cryo) elements present on the team to enable reactions and convert as many Shells as possible.

Chasca's bullets follow the 'last in first out' rule, meaning the last loaded bullet hits first, while the Anemo bullet in slot 1 hits last.

Chasca ideally needs 3 different types of PHEC (Pyro/Hydro/Electro/Cryo) to maximize the conversion rate and Damage Bonus from her passive Bullet Trick.

Ideal Combo: 4[CA6]. The number after 'CA' refers to the number of Bullets loaded

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Amos' Bow: As Chasca's Shining Shadowhunt Shells are not a projectile and therefore have no travel time, she does not benefit from any stacks of this weapon's passive. It is still a good Stat stick nonetheless.
Polar Star: In theory, this weapon performs slightly better than Amos' Bow. However, due to its high Crit Rate substat, optimizing builds around this weapon is very hard.
Scion of the Blazing Sun: At [R5], this weapon performs close to Skyward Harp.
Song of Stillness: Assumes permanent uptime on the passive. If the team cannot provide frequent heals, this weapon should not be considered.
Flower-Wreathed Feathers: At [R5], this weapon performs similarly to Skyward Harp.
Range Gauge: This weapon needs reliable healing to perform well. With frequent, guaranteed healing instances, this weapon performs slightly better than Song of Stillness [R5], and slightly better than Amos' Bow at [R5].

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Marechaussee Hunter: Only useful when paired with Furina, in which case it performs very close to Obsidian Codex (4).

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: Chasca generally has high Energy Recharge requirements. Building Energy Recharge to Burst every rotation is only feasible in energy rich teams or at [C4+].

builds.tips:
Chevreuse provides various character buffs and enemy debuffs when party members trigger Overload.

The main function of her kit, the Pyro and Electro RES reduction, only activates if the entire team consists of Pyro and Electro characters.

Her buff caps at 40,000 HP, after which she no longer needs to build HP.

Chevreuse does not always need to use her Burst, so Energy Recharge is not needed if she chooses not to use her Burst.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Symphonist of Scents: Bear in mind you will need around 31% HP from substat rolls with HP% / HP% / HP% main stats to cap out her A4 passive using this, which makes it difficult to build enough Energy Recharge to use her Burst every rotation.
Favonius Lance: Loses some value if Chevreuse does not Burst. Still valuable for team energy.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige: Only use this set if you use Chevreuse's Burst every rotation.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: Only if needed. Prioritize until meeting Energy Recharge requirements.

1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. DEF%
3. ATK%

builds.tips:
Chiori is an off-field damage dealer focusing on her automation dolls (Skill DMG). While there is an incentive to play her in teams which have a Geo Construct (pre-C1), she is still a good option even in Geo teams which do not have a Geo Construct (such as with Navia).

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Harbinger of Dawn: Ranking is assuming the passive is active. Chiori can usually have good uptime on the Harbinger of Dawn passive as she is primarily used as an off-field character, along with having good synergy with certain Geo characters like Zhongli who provide a shield. If using Furina however uptime may be problematic depending on how strong your teamwide healing is, or if facing enemies where corrosion is present.
Cinnabar Spindle: Ranking is assuming one doll. This weapon performs slightly worse than Harbinger of Dawn [R5] with 2 dolls.
Mistsplitter Reforged: Ranking assumes one stack on the passive.
Wolf-Fang: At [R5] this weapon performs close to Primordial Jade Cutter.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Golden Troupe: Outperforms Husk of Opulent Dreams (4) if you are ignoring Chiori's Burst completely. Husk of Opulent Dreams (4) can outperform Golden Troupe (4) if you are using Chiori's Burst more frequently.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: Chiori is not Burst reliant and can opt to Burst less often. It is not recommended to build ER% at the expense of offensive stats. ER% gains in value at [C2]+ when Bursting is needed to summon an additional doll.

builds.tips:
It is preferred to use Chongyun as a Burst Nuke over Main DPS as his Normal Attack scalings are very low, even when including [C1]. His Burst on the other hand hits extremely hard on later constellations and takes a much higher total damage portion over his Normal Attack. On top of that, you can Reverse Melt all 3 hits of his Burst on enemies applied with strong Pyro, such as Bennett's Skill and Burst.

At [C2], his Skill reduces ability cooldown that is cast within the zone by 15%. This is one of the few abilities in the game that can reduce cooldown.

Chongyun's Burst is calculated as Claymore damage, which means it breaks Geo Structures / Shields at a much easier rate and can proc Shatter.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Akuoumaru: At [R1], Akuoumaru beats Luxurious Sea-Lord if Chongyun is used in a team with 260 or greater total team energy. Akuoumaru only beats Luxurious Sea-Lord if [R2] and above otherwise.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige: Chongyun is one of the best Noblesse Oblige (4) holders since he has low energy requirements for his Burst.

builds.artifacts:
1. Fragment of Harmonic Whimsy (4)
2. Gladiator's Finale (4)
3. Thundering Fury (4)*
builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK% / Elemental Mastery
Goblet - Electro DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. ATK%
3. Elemental Mastery*
4. Energy Recharge*
builds.tips:
Clorinde is an Electro DPS who primarily deals damage with her Skill, which puts her into the Night Vigil state. Attacks during this state grant her a Bond of Life, and she can clear her Bond of Life by using her Skill again while in the Night Vigil state. It should be noted that while in the Night Vigil state, outside healing sources give Clorinde a Bond of Life instead of healing her. Clorinde's Burst also grants her a Bond of Life that increases based on her talent level.

Combo with Burst:
- Q E2 5N3E (N2 or N3)
- Q E 6N3E

Combo without Burst:
- E 6N3E

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Haran Geppaku Futsu: Be careful when using this weapon as it is easy to overcap on Crit Rate due to Clorinde's A4 talent giving her a free 20% Crit Rate.
Mistsplitter Reforged: Maximizing Stacks requires Clorinde to use her Burst before her Skill.
Primordial Jade Cutter: Be careful when using this weapon as it is easy to overcap on Crit Rate due to Clorinde's A4 talent giving her a free 20% Crit Rate.
Freedom-Sworn: This weapon loses significant value outside of Dendro-based teams.
Calamity of Eshu: Ranking assumes Clorinde is being shielded by someone, most notably being Kirara.
The Black Sword: At [R5], this weapon will perform better than Finale of the Deep [R5].

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Thundering Fury: This set pulls ahead of Fragment of Harmonic Whimsy if Clorinde is played in Quickbloom with Furina, and is competitive with Fragment of Harmonic Whimsy in Aggravate.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Elemental Mastery: In Quickbloom scenarios, Elemental Mastery has a similar priority to ATK%, and has a lower priority in Aggravate. It should not be built outside of Dendro-based teams.
Energy Recharge: Having Energy Recharge rolls can allow Clorinde to be able to Burst every rotation, which is useful, however, Energy Recharge should not be prioritized.

notes:

Attacks during this state grant her a Bond of Life, and she can clear her Bond of Life by using her Skill again while in the Night Vigil state. It should be noted that while in the Night Vigil state, outside healing sources give Clorinde a Bond of Life instead of healing her. Clorinde's Burst also grants her a Bond of Life that increases based on her talent level.

Collei is a Bow User who provides off-field Dendro application and some damage.

Typical Collei Rotation:

E > Q > Swap

Rotation with Polar Star:

N1 > tap CA > E > Q > Swap

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Polar Star: The best weapon for Collei's personal damage, however it is generally best to opt for a support weapon like Elegy for the End as the teamwide buff is more valuable.
Sacrificial Bow: Should only be used at at least [R4].

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: Collei's Sprout damage counts as Skill damage, which allows her to be able to keep Tenacity of the Millelith (4) up during important damage windows. Generally an alternative to Noblesse Oblige (4) or Deepwood Memories (4) (or if you are already running both in a team).
Gilded Dreams: This set is only used in a team where Collei is used to trigger reactions (i.e. Bloom/Burning teams).

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

Columbina blesses her team with a large buff to Lunar reactions, while also inflicting good amounts of Lunar damage herself, mimicking whatever lunar reaction is dominant in your team.

builds.tips:
[In-depth guide](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1YMPD8Cv0pMOiGENcAG5EzHJt4uQxRxJG0q6LLgv0FiA/edit?gid=0#gid=0) from Cyno Mains

Cyno is a DPS character that uses his Burst to change stance and deal Electro damage. The damage dealt during this stance is Normal Attack damage so Burst damage buffs will not affect him. Cyno in Aggravate focuses solely on doing high personal damage with his Burst stance with other Dendro and Electro characters.

Thundering Fury (4): E N1 Q E 6[N4E]

Non-Thundering Fury: E N1 Q N2E 3(N5 N2E)

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Ballad of the Fjords: Assumes that the passive is active. At [R5], this weapon performs slightly worse than Disaster and Remorse.
Deathmatch: At [R5], this weapon performs better than Primordial Jade Winged-Spear.
Skyward Spine: This weapon may perform equal to or better than White Tassel [R5] if the ATK Speed it gives is utilized to hit more of Cyno's Normal Attacks. In teams where energy is scarce, such as teams without another Favonius weapon or an Electro user, this weapon can be desired for the Energy Recharge it gives.
Kitain Cross Spear: In teams where energy is scarce, such as teams without another Favonius weapon or an Electro user, this weapon can be desired for the Energy Recharge it gives.
Favonius Lance: In teams where energy is scarce, such as teams without another Favonius weapon or an Electro user, this weapon can be desired for the Energy Recharge it gives.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Thundering Fury: In optimal conditions, this is Cyno's best artifact set. This set is an especially competitive option when Cyno is the only Electro character in the team, as the extra Skill casts help alleviate his energy issues. Do note that casting his Skill off cooldown with this set might lead to missing Endseer stance windows. It is highly recommended to use a shielder with Cyno with this set.
Gilded Dreams: This artifact set is better than Gladiator's Finale (4) on Staff of the Scarlet Sands and White Tassel.
Thundersoother: If you can guarantee 100% uptime on this set, it performs better than Gladiator's Finale (4). However, satisfying this condition is infeasible on most teams.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

Dahlia offers both shielding and an ATK SPD increase to characters protected by his shield, both of which scale with his HP%. As such, building Energy Recharge to use Dahlia's Burst and building HP% to increase his support capabilities are the focus of his build.

Dahlia's shield will take overflow damage when broken. This occurs before it is renewed with a Benison stack.

Dahlia's A4 passive is maximized at 40,000 HP.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Peak Patrol Song: Does not aid Dahlia's stats, but offer some team buffing in return. Only use it if the team can reliably trigger its passive, and you've met Energy Recharge and HP% thresholds.

2. Crit Rate / DMG

builds.tips:
On-Field DPS Dehya relies on using her Skill and Burst to do damage.

Dehya's Burst cannot trigger effects that trigger on Normal Attack animations or damage (e.g. Xingqiu, Beidou).

At [C1], HP% becomes a viable stat for Dehya competing with ATK% stats while providing survivability. Additionally, 5-star weapon stat sticks can be worse than some 4-star options as their high Base ATK stats matter less.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Serpent Spine: Dehya has a hard time keeping up this weapon's stacks. At [R5], this weapon can perform better than Wolf's Gravestone.
Mailed Flower: This weapon is only useful in Melt or Vaporize teams and should not be considered otherwise.
Rainslasher: Only useful in Vaporize teams. At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Redhorn Stonethresher.
Tidal Shadow: Assumes Dehya is being healed.
Talking Stick: Assumes Dehya is affected by Pyro. At [R5], this weapon performs better than Tidal Shadow [R5].
A Thousand Blazing Suns: As Dehya needs to place her Skill at the very start of the rotation to not suffer from very high Energy Recharge requirements, she often cannot have this weapon's passive active during her Burst. In teams where 10 seconds pass between her Skill and Burst use, this weapon performs similar to Redhorn Stonethresher.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Unfinished Reverie: This is Dehya's best set if played in a team with Burning present.
Marechaussee Hunter: With Furina on the team, this set performs similar to or better than Emblem of Severed Fate (4), depending on Dehya's Energy Recharge requirement.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Elemental Mastery: Desirable stat if Dehya is played in a Vaporize or Melt team.

1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. ATK%

4. Energy Recharge
builds.tips:
Diluc has a number of combos he can do during his uptime:

His most common combo is Q N2 E N2 E N2 E. This fits perfectly into the buff window of his teams.

For high upfront damage, use E Q N1 E N1 E N1. This falls off significantly if the enemy is not killed within this window.

A full rotation combo can look similar to Q N2 E N2 E N2 E N4 N3 E N1 E E. An example video can be found [here](https://www.google.com/url?q=https://youtu.be/wDzOoHiMpbs&sa=D&source=editors&ust=1778778522182526&usg=AOvVaw1P98QnGDq6o8zGpTOD7PtJ).

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Beacon of the Reed Sea: This weapon ranking assumes one stack.
Serpent Spine: Assumes you can maintain high stacks of the weapon effect. At [R5], it is Diluc's best weapon.
Rainslasher: Only use this weapon in teams where Diluc is consistently triggering Vaporize. It scales well with refinements, potentially surpassing Wolf's Gravestone at [R5].
Tidal Shadow: Assumes Diluc can consistently be healed. Otherwise, this weapon ranks below Earth Shaker.
Blackcliff Slasher: Assumes the weapon passive does not activate.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Crimson Witch of Flames: Generally the best go-to set for Diluc due to how easily he can stack the Pyro DMG effect, while his Burst imbues his Normal Attack and Charged Attack with Pyro.
Marechaussee Hunter: Competitive with Crimson Witch of Flames (4) when using Furina alongside Diluc.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Elemental Mastery: Higher priority in Vaporize or Melt teams.

name: Diona

builds.weapons:
1. Favonius Warbow (4✩)
2. Sacrificial Bow (4✩)*
3. Elegy for the End (5✩)
4. Silvershower Heartstrings (5✩)*
5. Recurve Bow (3✩)*
≈ Sequence of Solitude [5] (4✩)*
builds.artifacts:
1. Noblesse Oblige (4)*
≈ Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4)
2. +20% HP set (2) / +20% HP set (2) / +20% ER set (2) / +15% Healing Bonus set (2) [Choose Two]
3. Maiden Beloved (4)
≈ Ocean-Hued Clam (4)
4. Instructor (4)*

builds.main_stats:
Sands - Energy Recharge / HP%
Goblet - HP%
Circlet - HP% / Healing Bonus
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge
2. HP%
3. Crit Rate*
4. Flat HP
builds.tips:
As a support, Diona is typically used for three things: Shielding, Healing, and Cryo particle generation. Pairing Diona with Cryo damage dealers such as Kamisato Ayaka, Ganyu, and Eula allows you to funnel energy into them while providing them a shield. Weapons such as Sacrificial Bow and Favonius Warbow greatly increase her potential as a battery.

Diona's Skill grants a Cryo shield which allows cleansing. Every time you swap characters with the shield up, it applies Cryo. This acts as a cleanse.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sacrificial Bow: Preferred over Favonius Warbow at higher refinements (R3+) when batterying another Cryo character.
Silvershower Heartstrings: One of the best choice for stronger shields, however the Energy Recharge and useful passives of other weapons will generally make it a suboptimal pick.
Recurve Bow: One of the best choice for stronger shields, however the Energy Recharge and useful passives of other weapons will generally make it a suboptimal pick.
Sequence of Solitude: One of the best choice for stronger shields, however the Energy Recharge and useful passives of other weapons will generally make it a suboptimal pick.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige: This set provides a teamwide ATK% buff while also increasing Burst damage. Diona does not have the best uptime on this set, however, she may be the best option to use this set.
Instructor: This set is a niche option for Diona. It can be considered in teams which value Elemental Mastery and where she can trigger a reaction while on-field. Keep in mind that Instructor being limited to a 4-star set means your stat potential will be lower.
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set is a niche option for Diona. It can be considered in teams including some variants of Ganyu Freeze which have shorter rotation lengths, meaning Diona's Burst will not be used every rotation, and where you can snapshot the ATK% buff onto Ganyu's Burst.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. ATK%
3. Energy Recharge
builds.tips:
This playstyle uses Durin's Purity form to decrease the Elemental Resistance of Pyro, Dendro, Electro, Anemo and Geo when reactions with those elements and Pyro occur.

Purity form is Durin's default Burst, it can be swapped to (if in Denial form) via an E E Q rotation.

Durin's Burst in Purity form hits once per 1s, and can apply Pyro in an AoE every other hit.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Freedom-Sworn: This weapon is ranked assuming the passive can be activated AND the team benefits from the passive. Otherwise do not use this weapon.
Wolf-Fang: At [R5] this weapon ranks similarly to Azurelight.
Lion's Roar: This weapon is ranked assuming 90% uptime on the passive effect. At [R5], this weapon ranks similarly to Wolf-Fang.
Harbinger of Dawn: This weapon is ranked assuming the passive is always active. Otherwise, do not use this weapon.
Finale of the Deep: If you can reliably clear the Bond of Life, this weapon ranks similarly to Summit Shaper.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Celestial Gift: This set is usually Durin's best set on teams with Hexerei: Secret Rite if there is no one else on the team that can hold it. 
Emblem of Severed Fate: This set can be competitive over A Day Carved From Rising Winds (4) in teams where Durin has notably higher Energy Recharge Requirements (usually 160%+).

Emilie does not use the Burning reaction as her primary source of damage, but rather she uses the reaction to help her continuously deal Dendro damage. Thus, Emilie would rather build offensive stats rather than focus on building around the Burning reaction.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Symphonist of Scents: Keep in mind that while this weapon serves as a decent stat stick for Emilie, using it on her wastes one of the game's stronger buffing weapons since her kit lacks any healing to activate its buff.
Deathmatch: At [R5], this weapon will perform better than Engulfing Lightning, and it will perform a little worse than Staff of Homa if she is played in single-target.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Deepwood Memories: This set has significantly less value on Emilie if someone else in the team can hold it.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: Emilie does not need to Burst every rotation in some teams, so Energy Recharge becomes less important.

builds.tips:
Escoffier is a DPS that dishes out respectable amounts of Off-Field damage through her Skill while also serving up a sizeable Cryo and Hydro Resistance shred thanks to her Ascension 4 Talent. Additionally, her Burst and Ascension 1 Talent allow her to keep her Team well nourished and healed.

Escoffier's Hold Skill can provide you with up to 10 special delicious dishes per week, but does not help you with cooking your enemies, so avoid using it during combat.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Deathmatch: Ranking assumes one enemy. At [R5], this weapon performs better than Staff of Homa.
Favonius Lance: While inferior to a number of weapons in terms of personal damage, this weapon can be very good thanks to helping the team with Energy.
Sacrificer's Staff: At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Engulfing Lightning.
Prospector's Drill: Escoffier can reliably get high stacks starting from second rotation onward. Performs close to Lumidouce Elegy at [R5].
Mountain-Bracing Bolt: At [R5] this weapon performs slightly better than similar ranking options.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Blizzard Strayer: Against freezable enemies, this set slightly outperforms Golden Troupe (4), however it is way worse without reliable Freeze uptime and can be hard to build around with Crit Rate weapons.
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set is very bad for Escoffier's personal damage, but provide an additional buff to the team.
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: This set is very bad for Escoffier's personal damage, but provide an additional buff to the team.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

Prospector's Drill: Escoffier can reliably get high stacks starting from second rotation onward. Performs better than Staff of the Scarlet Sands at [R5].
Mountain-Bracing Bolt: At [R5] this weapon performs slightly better than similar ranking options.
Favonius Lance: This weapon is extremely detrimental for Escoffier's personal damage if she does not have high Energy Recharge Requirements, but can possibly yield more team damage.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Blizzard Strayer: Against freezable enemies, this set slightly outperforms Golden Troupe (4), however it is way worse without reliable Freeze uptime and can be hard to build around with Crit Rate weapons.
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set is very bad for Escoffier's personal damage, but provide an additional buff to the team.
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: This set is very bad for Escoffier's personal damage, but provide an additional buff to the team.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

notes:
Escoffier is a DPS that dishes out respectable amounts of Off-Field damage through her Skill while also serving up a sizeable Cryo and Hydro Resistance shred thanks to her Ascension 4 Talent. Additionally, her Burst and Ascension 1 Talent allow her to keep her Team well nourished and healed.

Escoffier's Hold Skill can provide you with up to 10 special delicious dishes per week, but does not help you with cooking your enemies, so avoid using it during combat.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Deathmatch: Ranking assumes one enemy. At [R5], this weapon performs better than Staff of Homa.
Favonius Lance: While inferior to a number of weapons in terms of personal damage, this weapon can be very good thanks to helping the team with Energy.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK%
Goblet - Physical DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. Energy Recharge
3. ATK%
builds.tips:
Eula is an on-field Physical DMG dealer who deals a significant portion of her damage through her Burst. Her Normal Attacks also make up a large portion of her overall damage at low Constellation. Weapon rankings are assuming 130% Energy Recharge minimum, Superconduct and 4pc Noblesse Oblige buff, otherwise no buffs.

Normal combo while no Burst: NA Spam > tE > N Spam > tE

Burst combo: tE Q N4 hE N4

Burst combo that generates more energy at the cost of slightly less DPS: tE Q tE N4 D N2 hE N2

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Beacon of the Reed Sea: Assumes one stack. At two stacks, it outperforms other weapon options.
Verdict: Weapon rankings are assuming no Crystallize.
The Unforged: Weapon rankings are assuming no shield. With a shield this weapon performs similarly to Beacon of the Reed Sea and Wolf's Gravestone.
Redhorn Stonethresher: This weapon can overtake Wolf's Gravestone in terms of Eula's own damage if team provides enough ATK buffs, but Wolf's Gravestone can make up the damage difference by buffing team damage.
Serpent Spine: Weapon rankings are assuming max stacks, in practice this often means running a shielder. At [R5] this weapon performs similarly to Beacon of the Reed Sea.
Akuoumaru: This weapon will outperform Luxurious Sea-Lord at higher refinements. At [R5] it can perform similarly to Verdict and Skyward Pride.
Tidal Shadow: Weapon rankings are assuming the passive is active. At [R5] this weapon performs similarly to Serpent Spine [R1] at 5 stacks.
Lithic Blade: Assumes 1 stack.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Pale Flame: This is Eula's optimal artifact set and it relies on having a condition fulfilled in order to outperform Pale Flame (2) Bloodstained Chivalry (2). If you are able to sustain the conditional buff, you will be able to achieve a 50% Physical DMG Bonus + 18% ATK. Without using your Burst, you can sustain the Pale Flame's 4 piece set bonus because each stack lasts 7s and it refreshes per stack, and Eula's Skill cooldown is only 4 seconds when doing 1-taps, therefore you can keep the condition up. When using this set, it is recommended to not use Hold Skill cast until your Lightfall Sword is nearly about to end. This restriction is only for players who don't have Eula [C2+].
Bloodstained Chivalry: With Pale Flame (2), this is a non-conditional, consistent artifact set that provides you a constant 50% Physical DMG bonus. While that by itself is amazing, Pale Flame (4) can outperform this set because it also has another 18% ATK buff and is easily maintained through her low tap Skill cooldown.

name: Faruzan

builds.weapons:
1. Favonius Warbow (4✩)
2. Elegy for the End (5✩)*
3. Snare Hook (4✩)
4. End of the Line (4✩)
5. Fading Twilight (4✩)
≈ Sacrificial Bow (4✩)*
builds.artifacts:
1. Noblesse Oblige (4)*
≈ Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4)
2. Viridescent Venerer (4)*
3. Emblem of Severed Fate (4)*
4. Golden Troupe (4)*
≈ Tenacity of the Millelith (4)*
5. +20% ER set (2) / +20% ER set (2)*
builds.main_stats:
Sands - Energy Recharge
Goblet - Anemo DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge
2. Crit Rate*
3. Crit DMG
4. ATK%
builds.tips:
As the premier Anemo support unit for Anemo dps based teams, Faruzan combines both high Anemo damage buffing with Anemo resistance shredding to become a cornerstone of any team that wants to focus on Anemo damage. Though building her means primarily focusing on dealing with her high Energy Recharge Requirements.

For funneling particles to Faruzan: E C Q

For funneling particles to other characters: E Q C

Bear in mind that at [C6] the Charged shot portion of the rotation becomes no longer necessary since your Burst will start activating the Pressurized Collapses instead.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Elegy for the End: Usually only a better option at higher constellation levels such as [C6] where her energy needs can be met without Favonius passive particles.
Sacrificial Bow: It's worth mentioning that the extra Pressurized Collapse from a second Skill wont give you any extra particles if it's within her 5.5 second particle generation cooldown.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige: This is her best option in teams that either won't benefit significantly from Viridescent Venerer (4) shred, or teams that already have another unit holding the aforementioned set.
Viridescent Venerer: This set should be used in teams which have a significant amount of Pyro/Hydro/Electro/Cryo damage. Note the resistance shred does not buff Anemo damage and the set wielder must Swirl the element(s) while on-field for the resistance shred.
Emblem of Severed Fate: This set will give Faruzan her best personal damage output, assuming the team is already running Viridescent Venerer (4). Also if you're willing to invest more time farming substats it can potentially give you more Energy Recharge than running The Exile (2).
Golden Troupe: This set is better than Emblem of Severed Fate (4) if Faruzan is [C6] because of the pressurized collapse damage.
Tenacity of the Millelith: This has similar usage cases to Noblesse Oblige (4), except in that it requires Faruzan [C6] for improved uptime through extra Pressurized Collapse activations.
+20% ER set: Faruzan's Energy Recharge requirements pre-[C6] can be extremely high, so high that it can be difficult to build enough using a 4pc set bonus. As such, using Emblem of Severed Fate (2) with whatever artifact combination will give you the most Energy Recharge is a reasonable option.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: Only assumes higher priority than Crit DMG when using Favonius Warbow.

Flins is an on-field DPS unit who utilizes a short field time to generate energy for himself and use the lower Energy cost Burst when possible. Because most of his damage is Lunar-Charged, Electro DMG% and other DMG% buffs are not particularly useful for him.

Flins requires another Moonsign character in his team to unlock a large portion of his damage potential.

Regarding Weapon Choices:

Deathmatch: At [R5], this weapon performs better than Crimson Moon's Semblance.
Ballad of the Fjords: At [R5], this weapon performs better than Crimson Moon's Semblance.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:

Furina buffs team members with her Burst, and deals off-field damage with her Skill.

Regarding Weapon Choices:

builds.tips:
Ganyu's Burst is the main damage source in Freeze teams, therefore Charged Attacks should only be used when the time between using the teams Skills and Bursts allows it. If running Shenhe alongside Ganyu, weapons with Crit Rate / DMG stats and/or DMG% are slightly favored.

With Blizzard Strayer (4), it is best to aim close to but not above 45% Crit Rate.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Polar Star: This weapon is much harder to optimize with, due to having a very high Crit Rate stat.
Amos' Bow: In quickswap heavy rotations (Ganyu Mona Venti Diona) where you usually don't do a lot of Charged Attacks, this weapon performs worse than the other 5 star options listed.
Song of Stillness: Ranking assumed Ganyu receives healing before casting her Burst.
Prototype Crescent: Requires a weak spot hit before casting Ganyu's Burst to utilize the passive fully. At [R5], this weapon peforms close to Skyward Harp.
Mouun's Moon: This weapon's exact performance varies with the teams total Burst cost. At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Thundering Pulse.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Marechaussee Hunter: This set should only be used with Furina on the team and performs only slightly worse than Blizzard Strayer (4).

builds.tips:
Ganyu's Level 2 Charged Attack applies two instances Cryo as they do not share the same ICD, which allows you to trigger Melt reaction twice for both damage instances. Charged Attack will be the majority of her damage output while neglecting her Skill/Burst damage contribution for most scenarios.

Ganyu's Burst should not be used in most Melt teams, as it can lead to Charged Attacks not being able to trigger Melt.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Amos' Bow: Ganyu's Level 2 Charged Attack Bloom extends arrow flight duration allowing her to get 3 stacks of Amos' Bow passive even on point blank range.
Flower-Wreathed Feathers: This weapon scales incredibly well with refinements. At [R5], this weapon performs better than The First Great Magic.
Hamayumi: Due to its passive, this weapon prefers Wanderer's Troupe (4) instead. At higher refinements, Hamayumi can outperform Thundering Pulse and Skyward Harp.
Scion of the Blazing Sun: At [R5], this weapon can outperform Thundering Pulse and Skyward Harp.
Prototype Crescent: At higher refinement levels with good uptime on its passive, it can potentially be on par with Skyward Harp. However, do bear in mind that not all enemies have weak spots that you can headshot, which does affect its performance significantly.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Shimenawa's Reminiscence: If you are able to squeeze 5 Charged Attacks during the 10 second buff duration, Shimenawa's Reminiscence (4) pulls ahead of Wanderer's Troupe (4). Due to it being very difficult to do, Wanderer's Troupe (4) will usually end up dealing more damage for most players.
Unfinished Reverie: If Ganyu is played in a team with consistent Burning uptime, this set performs better than Wanderer's Troupe (4) and Shimenawa's Reminiscence (4).

Sands - Energy Recharge
Goblet - Geo DMG / DEF%
Circlet - Crit Rate / DEF% / Healing Bonus
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge
2. DEF%
3. Crit Rate
builds.tips:
Gorou is a Support character that can give increasing buffs to your party members from his Skill and Burst based on the number of Geo characters in your team (capped at 3). Casting his Skill sets up a banner which gives Flat DEF (1 Geo), Interruption Resistance (2 Geo) and Geo DMG Bonus (3 Geo) for your active character standing inside the banner radius.

Casting his Burst creates a field following the active character which deals periodic damage and offers the same bonuses given by the Skill, Destroying Any Placed Banners In The Process, It Also Pulls In Nearby Elemental Shards Created By element:crystallize reactions.

Due to his kit focusing on buffing DEF% and Geo DMG, he's a specialised support geared towards Geo DPS that scales with DEF, such as Noelle or Arataki Itto. He can see niche use at [C6] with other Geo characters as well.

[C2] extends the duration of Gorou's Burst from 9 seconds to 12 seconds if you can obtain the required Crystallize shields. This allows his teammates to gain the 3 Geo 15% Geo DMG bonus for a longer duration, so it's preferable to use Burst as your last ability before swapping into your DPS.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Elegy for the End: Offers a helpful Energy Recharge substat alongside an Elemental Mastery and ATK% buff for your teammates. While not directly useful in teams centered around Noelle or Arataki Itto, this could possibly be a good choice for future Geo DPS characters, or in a team which utilizes Geo Resonance.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Instructor: This is a set Gorou may run when used in a Lunar-Crystallize team, notably when paired with Zibai. Because this is a 4-star set, you're losing out on 5-star stat scaling. It is recommended to run an offpiece 5-star sands while running this set.
Silken Moon's Serenade: Can be used when Gorou is paired with Columbina or Zibai so that other units can run more beneficial artifact sets.

builds.tips:
Shikanoin Heizou's Anemo DPS build is roughly equal to his Elemental Mastery build assuming he isn't being used in an Electro-Charged team. He has very low Energy Recharge needs; At [C4] he can run 100% Energy Recharge.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Lost Prayer to the Sacred Winds: In teams where Shikanoin Heizou and Bennett are used in the same team, this weapon is better than Skyward Atlas.
The Widsith: This weapon provides the highest possible critical hit single strike damage for Shikanoin Heizou. However, its long cooldown leads it to lack consistency, and it has a chance of obtaining the relatively useless Elemental Mastery buff for Anemo DPS Shikanoin Heizou.
Favonius Codex: This weapon can help lower Energy Recharge requirements for the team, as well as allow Shikanoin Heizou to run less Energy Recharge on himself.
Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers: This weapon offers a colossal 48% ATK buff to a party member at the expense of Shikanoin Heizou's damage.
Hakushin Ring: In Electro-Charged teams, this can be used to buff both Anemo, Hydro, and Electro damage, and scales well with refines. It is recommended to run Anemo DMG based artifacts on this build.
Prototype Amber: In teams where there is no healer, Shikanoin Heizou can use this weapon to help the team's survivability.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Viridescent Venerer: Shikanoin Heizou should use this set unless someone else in the team has it.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - HP% / Elemental Mastery
Goblet - Pyro DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. Elemental Mastery*
3. HP%
4. ATK%
builds.tips:
Hu Tao is a predominantly Charged Attack focused DPS who best synergizes in teams with sufficient off-field Hydro application which allow her to Vaporize all of her Charged Attack hits.

When ending Hu Tao's combos with a Burst, if you do not jump/dash cancel the last Charged Attack, the Burst still benefit from Hu Tao's Skill.

It is recommended to use cancels when performing your Hu Tao combos as to maximize damage.

N2CJ for [C0] and N2CD for [C1]. N3CJ/N3CD can be used to conserve stamina at the cost of damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Staff of the Scarlet Sands: When using this weapon, Gilded Dreams (4) becomes competitive with Crimson Witch of Flames (4) for being Hu Tao's best artifact set.
Ballad of the Fjords: At [R5], this weapon is better than Staff of the Scarlet Sands.
Deathmatch: This weapon is better than Dragon's Bane when you're not doing Vaporize.
Primordial Jade Winged-Spear: Is better than the [R1] variants of Dragon's Bane and Deathmatch with ideal stacking. While this is a very strong weapon when fully stacked, maintaining the stacks on Hu Tao isn't ideal in realistic situations making the weapon less consistent than other options.
Lithic Spear: At [R5], this weapon is better than Staff of the Scarlet Sands.
White Tassel: This weapon prefers an N2C combo over an N1C combo.
Blackcliff Pole: Despite seeming quite good due to the Crit DMG substat, Blackcliff Pole at 1 stack performs only slightly better than an [R5] White Tassel in practice as it is difficult to maintain stacks consistently. It is not recommended to buy this weapon specifically for Hu Tao and should only be used if you already have one.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Shimenawa's Reminiscence: Provides higher Normal Attack and Charged Attack damage than Crimson Witch of Flames (4), but reduces Burst damage, impacting overall AoE damage. Additionally, if you're unable to Burst, it might lead to survivability issues unless you're running a healer.
Gilded Dreams: At 3 Elemental Mastery stacks, this artifact set is equal to Crimson Witch of Flames (4) in teams where you are not getting Elemental Mastery from other sources (e.g team or weapon).
Marechaussee Hunter: This is Hu Tao's best set when you can reliably get 3 stacks, which typically requires running Furina, or healer that can heal smaller amounts over time (i.e. Xianyun, Chevreuse).

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Elemental Mastery: Since Hu Tao is able to trigger Vaporize on most of her damage output, Elemental Mastery is quite valuable on her. If you have less than 100EM total (including from Sucrose's Elemental Mastery share, Instructor (4), etc) Elemental Mastery substats take priority over Crit Rate / DMG.

notes:
Hu Tao is a predominantly Charged Attack focused DPS who best synergizes in teams with sufficient off-field Hydro application which allow her to Vaporize all of her Charged Attack hits.

When ending Hu Tao's combos with a Burst, if you do not jump/dash cancel the last Charged Attack, the Burst still benefit from Hu Tao's Skill.

It is recommended to use cancels when performing your Hu Tao combos as to maximize damage.

N2CJ for [C0] and N2CD for [C1]. N3CJ/N3CD can be used to conserve stamina at the cost of damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Staff of the Scarlet Sands: When using this weapon, Gilded Dreams (4) becomes competitive with Crimson Witch of Flames (4) for being Hu Tao's best artifact set.
Ballad of the Fjords: At [R5], this weapon is better than Staff of the Scarlet Sands.
Deathmatch: This weapon is better than Dragon's Bane when you're not doing Vaporize.
Primordial Jade Winged-Spear: Is better than the [R1] variants of Dragon's Bane and Deathmatch with ideal stacking. While this is a very strong weapon when fully stacked, maintaining the stacks on Hu Tao isn't ideal in realistic situations making the weapon less consistent than other options.
Lithic Spear: At [R5], this weapon is better than Staff of the Scarlet Sands.
White Tassel: This weapon prefers an N2C combo over an N1C combo.
Blackcliff Pole: Despite seeming quite good due to the Crit DMG substat, Blackcliff Pole at 1 stack performs only slightly better than an [R5] White Tassel in practice as it is difficult to maintain stacks consistently. It is not recommended to buy this weapon specifically for Hu Tao and should only be used if you already have one.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Shimenawa's Reminiscence: Provides higher Normal Attack and Charged Attack damage than Crimson Witch of Flames (4), but reduces Burst damage, impacting overall AoE damage. Additionally, if you're unable to Burst, it might lead to survivability issues unless you're running a healer.
Gilded Dreams: At 3 Elemental Mastery stacks, this artifact set is equal to Crimson Witch of Flames (4) in teams where you are not getting Elemental Mastery from other sources (e.g team or weapon).
Marechaussee Hunter: This is Hu Tao's best set when you can reliably get 3 stacks, which typically requires running Furina, or healer that can heal smaller amounts over time (i.e. Xianyun, Chevreuse).

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Elemental Mastery: Since Hu Tao is able to trigger Vaporize on most of her damage output, Elemental Mastery is quite valuable on her. If you have less than 100EM total (including from Sucrose's Elemental Mastery share, Instructor (4), etc) Elemental Mastery substats take priority over Crit Rate / DMG.

Goblet - ATK%
Circlet - ATK% / Crit Rate

1. Energy Recharge*
2. ATK%
3. Crit Rate*
builds.tips:
Iansan's buff is based on 3 Factors:

1. Iansan's Total ATK
2. # of Iansan's Nightsoul Point
3. "Kinetic Energy Scale"

Iansan can hold a max of 54 Nightsoul Point, and her buff decreases in value when she's below 42 Nightsoul Point. Consistent movement is needed to maintain good buff uptime.

Iansan's [C4] allows her to frontload Nightsoul Point if it had ever overflowed previously.

Iansan buffs her teammates through her Burst, as well as provide some additional healing for the active character through one of her passives. Characters with movement-based abilities will greatly contribute to Iansan's buff as Iansan's own movement cannot contribute to it. Movement is needed to help Iansan maintain a high number of Nightsoul Point, and the active character gaining and consuming Nightsoul Point can also contribute.

Movement is defined as anything that moves the active character for any amount of distance, whether vertical or horizontal. This includes things such as attacks, Skill and Burst animations, jumping, etc. Almost any kind of movement will contribute, as long as it's not Iansan's own. This also extends to the gain and consumption of Nightsoul Point.

Iansan's [C1] gives her 15 Energy upon consuming 6 Nightsoul Point as long as she's in combat and in Nightsoul Blessing state, effectively changing her Burst cost from 70 to 55. This has a cooldown of 18 seconds.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Symphonist of Scents: Keep in mind that the fewer Natlan characters you have on your team, the lower the buff's uptime will be with near perfect uptime usually only achievable when using three or more Natlan characters.
Favonius Lance: favo.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

builds.tips:
Illuga buffs Geo DMG and Lunar-Crystallize DMG after using his Burst, which grants the Nightingale's Song effect. This buff scales on Illuga's Elemental Mastery, and with his A4 talent, is further amplified depending on how many Geo and Hydro characters are in the party.

With his A1 talent, using either his Skill or Burst will grant other team members the Lightkeeper's Oath effect, which boosts the Crit Rate and Crit DMG of Geo DMG, and if Ascendant Gleam is active, boost the character's Elemental Mastery as well.

Lightkeeper's Oath does not apply to Illuga himself.

Nightingale's Song starts with 21 stacks, and for every Geo Construct that is on the field when his Burst is used, or if Geo Constructs are created during his Burst, Nightingale's Song gains 5 stacks, up to a maximum of 15 additional stacks.

The Moondrifts that are created by the Lunar-Crystallize reaction count as Geo Constructs.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Dragon's Bane: An Energy Recharge sands may be necessary if you are unable to get enough ER from substats alone.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

builds.tips:
Ineffa transforms all instances of the Electro-Charged reaction into Lunar-Charged while she's in the team, which has its own scalings and can even crit. Both her Skill and Burst deploy her robot companion, Birgitta, to the field, who can deal damage and apply Electro while off-field. Additionally, Ineffa's Skill can also provide a shield. Her Burst also provides an Elemental Mastery buff based on a portion of Ineffa's ATK (her A4 talent).

Birgitta stays on-field longer than both of Ineffa's Skill and Burst individual cooldowns, meaning that Birgitta has 100% field uptime.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Staff of the Scarlet Sands: Can sometimes utilize Elemental Mastery Main Stats.
Deathmatch: Ranking assumes single target scenarios. At [R5], this weapon performs equal to Bloodsoaked Ruins.
Ballad of the Fjords: At [R5] this weapon will perform closer to Primordial Jade Winged-Spear.
Prospector's Shovel: Performs slightly worse than Skyward Spine outside of Ascendant Gleam teams.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: This is a more supportive artifact set that sacrifices Ineffa's personal damage in favor of an ATK% buff and an increase to her shield strength.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: When Ineffa has higher Energy Recharge requirements (typically 140%+), building ER to use her Burst every rotation results in a personal damage loss in exchange for more uptime on her teamwide buff. Teams with Ineffa as a significant source of damage will want to use her Burst every other rotation and ignore ER.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - DEF%
Goblet - Geo DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. Crit Rate / DMG
3. DEF%
4. ATK%
builds.tips:
Arataki Itto is a Geo DPS Character who takes advantage of his Burst's ATK boost and Geo infusion to deal significant damage during its uptime. His combos involve stacking and unleashing his Charged Attacks, named Arataki Kesagiri. Arataki Kesagiri consumes no stamina and can be cast continuously as long as he has Superlative Superstrength stacks. Superlative Superstrength is capped at 5 stacks at any given time, and Arataki Itto gets those stacks when his 2nd and 4th Normal Attack string hits an enemy. Activating his Burst allows his 1st and 3rd Normal Attack to also generate stacks. Arataki Itto's Skill deals damage to enemies on cast and summons a taunt, Ushi, which is considered a Geo Construct. This initial cast damage grants Arataki Itto 1 Superlative Superstrength stack, and upon Ushi leaving the field, Arataki Itto is granted another stack. When Ushi takes damage, Arataki Itto gains a stack (capped at 1 stack per 2 seconds). Arataki Itto's Burst converts his Normal and Charged Attacks into Geo DMG, increases Arataki Itto's Normal Attack Speed and converts a portion of Arataki Itto's current DEF to ATK.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Serpent Spine: This weapon scales great with refines on Arataki Itto,  making it way better than similar ranking options at [R5]. When using Husk of Opulent Dreams, Serpent Spine stacks are prioritized over Husk of Opulent Dreams stacks due to their conflicting nature in gaining stacks (On-Field vs Off-Field).
Whiteblind: Your rotations often start with 1 stack of this weapon, and due to Arataki Itto's Burst snapshotting DEF on cast, you're unable to take advantage of further DEF% from the stacks.
Skyward Pride: Can be a great or bad weapon for Arataki Itto depending on your ER needs. The less Energy Recharge you need (due to particle generation from your party members or ER from subs), the less useful this weapon becomes.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Retracing Bolide: This set grants you an additional 40% Normal and Charged Attack DMG when shielded. Crystallize can be unreliable so a consistent shielder is preferred, such as Zhongli.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

builds.tips:
Jean's Skill can be used to fling smaller mobs into the air to deal fall damage, and to stagger bigger ones. Her healing scales with ATK.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Amenoma Kageuchi: This weapon can lower your Energy Recharge requirements, depending on the number of stacks on the passive and the refinement of this weapon. As such, at higher refines / more stacks on the passive, this weapon can perform better than Skyward Blade, while at lower refines / less stacks, it can perform worse.
Lion's Roar: Depending on passive uptime, it can be ranked much higher in terms of Jean's personal damage.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Viridescent Venerer: The bread and butter for Anemo characters in general, as it is commonly used for the 40% elemental resistance shred.
Noblesse Oblige: Another option for providing utilities to the team. This set gives a 20% partywide ATK boost after Jean casts her Burst.
Ocean-Hued Clam: The best set for Jean's personal damage, as the damage it deals scales with Jean's Burst healing. However, because Jean's total damage output is on the low end, it is generally recommended to prioritize utility options such as Noblesse Oblige (4) or Viridescent Venerer (4) over this set.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

Reaction DPS Jean relies on her unique interaction regarding her Burst. If the active character if afflicted by Hydro / Pyro / Electro / Cryo, Jean's Burst will infuse them with Anemo and create a Swirl trigger around the character. This interaction can be abused by using Bennett to consistently infuse the active character with Pyro. This allows for an extremely high amount of Pyro Swirl triggers. These Swirl's damage scales off of Jean's Elemental Mastery.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

notes:
Jean's Skill can be used to fling smaller mobs into the air to deal fall damage, and to stagger bigger ones. Her healing scales with ATK.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Amenoma Kageuchi: This weapon can lower your Energy Recharge requirements, depending on the number of stacks on the passive and the refinement of this weapon. As such, at higher refines / more stacks on the passive, this weapon can perform better than Skyward Blade, while at lower refines / less stacks, it can perform worse.
Lion's Roar: Depending on passive uptime, it can be ranked much higher in terms of Jean's personal damage.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Viridescent Venerer: The bread and butter for Anemo characters in general, as it is commonly used for the 40% elemental resistance shred.
Noblesse Oblige: Another option for providing utilities to the team. This set gives a 20% partywide ATK boost after Jean casts her Burst.
Ocean-Hued Clam: The best set for Jean's personal damage, as the damage it deals scales with Jean's Burst healing. However, because Jean's total damage output is on the low end, it is generally recommended to prioritize utility options such as Noblesse Oblige (4) or Viridescent Venerer (4) over this set.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
