≈ Tenacity of the Millelith (4)
builds.main_stats:
Sands - DEF% / Energy Recharge
Goblet - Geo DMG / DEF%
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. Crit Rate / DMG
3. DEF%
builds.tips:
As a free unit from Natlan, Kachina's value comes from being able to provide Off-Field Geo Application with her Skill, and being able to hold Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4) to great effect. Kachina's Burst can extend the radius in which Turbo Twirly creates shockwaves, allowing you to hit enemies from a distance if you need to. While she can be built for damage, her personal scalings are rather low, and building her for damage isn't a requirement.

Kachina's [C1] allows her to restore 3 Energy whenever she picks up a Crystallize Shard, and mounting/dismounting Turbo Twirly absorbs nearby shards. The energy restoration can only trigger once every 5 seconds, but does help alleviate some of her Energy Recharge needs.

Turbo Twirly is considered a Geo Construct.

Kachina's Burst can bring Turbo Twirly back to her location if it is already on the field, allowing for a quick method of repositioning without having to actually ride Turbo Twirly. Her [C2] will summon a new Turbo Twirly if one isn't on the field instead.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Footprint of the Rainbow: Kachina can opt to use this weapon if her Energy Recharge needs are satisfied and the team does not value the energy from Favonius Lance. Refinements are unnecessary unless you wish to see more out of Kachina's personal damage.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

Goblet - Elemental Mastery
Circlet - Elemental Mastery

1. Energy Recharge*
2. Elemental Mastery

builds.tips:
Kaedehara Kazuha's 4th ascension passive provides an elemental DMG bonus buff depending on his Elemental Mastery, putting the main focus on his build on stacking Elemental Mastery while also having enough Energy Recharge to Burst every rotation, as his Burst extends the duration of his passive and deals respectable damage through Swirls.

The element which is absorbed by Kaedehara Kazuha's Burst follows the priority of Pyro > Hydro > Electro > Cryo, and can be absorbed from both the active character and enemies. (i.e. Standing in Bennett's Burst will result in Kaedehara Kazuha's Burst infusing Pyro as Bennett's Burst applies Pyro to the active character).

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Sword: favo
Xiphos' Moonlight: At [R1], this weapon usually performs worse than Favonius Sword due to providing less effective energy to the team. At higher refinements, this weapon can rival Freedom-Sworn in terms of value due to how much Energy Recharge it provides to Kaedehara Kazuha and the team.
Sacrificial Sword: This weapon is mainly used for the Energy Recharge it provides. The Skill reset should not be used unless the extra grouping is needed.
Iron Sting: This weapon can perform better than Sacrificial Sword if you can get enough Energy Recharge from substats.
Toukabou Shigure: This weapon can perform better than Sacrificial Sword if you can get enough Energy Recharge from substats.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Thundering Fury: This set is only used for a few specific teams, in which Kaedehara Kazuha is used on field to deal damage through Aggravate Swirl hits.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

Sands - ATK%
Goblet - Dendro DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. ATK%
3. Energy Recharge
builds.tips:
Kinich is a Dendro DPS who relies on tethering himself to enemies with his grappling hook and firing Loop Shots to generate Nightsoul Points in order to use the Scalespiker Cannon. Both the Loop Shots and the Scalespiker Cannon are considered Skill DMG. With his A1 Talent, his Skill puts enemies in the Desolation State, which, when they are affected by Burning or Burgeon, generates even more Nightsoul Points for Kinich. Summoning Ajaw to the field will extend his Nightsoul Blessing state briefly.

When paired with Xiangling, spin counter clockwise so her Pyronado can hit opponents more frequently.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Serpent Spine: Performs much worse without a shielder. At [R5], this weapon performs better than Beacon of the Reed Sea.
Earth Shaker: Earth Shaker is an inconsistent weapon choice in teams only consisting of Dendro and Pyro characters. Since only the activation of Burning counts toward the passive, meaning that the passive activation can only trigger once per enemy.
Wolf's Gravestone: This option is mainly just a statstick for Kinich as many of his teammates, barring Xiangling, Emilie, Mavuika, or Xingqiu, don't benefit much from the ATK% buff from the passive.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Obsidian Codex: This set is about equal to Unfinished Reverie (4). The more DMG% Kinich already has, the better this set performs.
Unfinished Reverie: This set is about equal to Obsidian Codex (4).  The less DMG% Kinich has from his weapon and/or team, the better this set performs. 
Marechaussee Hunter: Only to be used when paired with Furina, or when played in Burgeon. Performs similarly to Unfinished Reverie (4).

Unfinished Reverie: This set is about equal to Obsidian Codex (4).  The less DMG% Kinich has from his weapon and/or team, the better this set performs. 
Marechaussee Hunter: Only to be used when paired with Furina, or when played in Burgeon. Performs similarly to Unfinished Reverie (4).

Kirara can support the team by providing shields. She has the option to remain off-field for the majority of rotations, or stay onfield to drive and apply Dendro.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sapwood Blade: While worse than some other options in terms of shield strength, the Leaf of Consciousness buff can make it a stronger option overall.
Favonius Sword: Kirara's Energy Recharge requirements are generally low, however the passive can still be useful for generating energy for the rest of your team.
Freedom-Sworn: An option in teams which value the buff, though there are generally better users of this weapon.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Instructor: While worse for shield strength, this set is a great option when using Kirara in Aggravate teams for the teamwide Elemental Mastery buff.
Deepwood Memories: This set should be used in teams which involve a relevant amount of Dendro damage and nobody else is using it.
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set is an option when using Kirara on-field.

name: Klee

builds.weapons:
1. Reliquary of Truth (5✩)
2. Lost Prayer to the Sacred Winds (5✩)*
≈ Kagura's Verity (5✩)*
≈ Vivid Notions (5✩)
3. Tome of the Eternal Flow (5✩)
≈ Cashflow Supervision (5✩)
4. Tulaytullah's Remembrance (5✩)*
≈ Surf's Up (5✩)*
5. Skyward Atlas (5✩)
6. The Widsith (4✩)*
≈ Solar Pearl (4✩)*
7. Sacrificial Jade (4✩)
8. Flowing Purity [5] (4✩)*
builds.artifacts:
1. A Day Carved From Rising Winds (4)
2. Lavawalker (4)*
3. Echoes of an Offering (4)*
≈ Desert Pavilion Chronicle (4)*
4. +18% ATK set (2) / Crimson Witch of Flames (2) / Marechaussee Hunter (2) [Choose Two]

Fragment of Harmonic Whimsy (4)*
builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK%
Goblet - Pyro DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. Crit Rate / DMG
3. ATK%
builds.tips:
Being extremely squishy and prone to interruptions, Klee requires good positioning or a shielder.

This is partially mitigated in her best teams, which currently focus on Overload with Chevreuse and provide strong stagger, helping Klee avoid getting hit. Even with this consideration, her best teams nomally don't include a shielder, she can often get knocked around and even KO'd in the midst of your rotations.

Running a shielder makes survivability with Klee more comfortable, but at the cost of total DPS. You can choose teammates depending on your level of skill and comfortability.

Klee has access to several animation cancels that let her deal damage with her talents faster than normal.

Her A1 grants Explosive Spark stacks when she uses Normal Attack, Skill, or Burst. When consumed, they give her Charged Attack extra DMG% and prevent them from consuming Stamina.

Hexerei effects give her up to 150% DMG to her Enhanced Charged Attack. This effect should always be active at max stacks.

Her optimal combos include N1C/N3 in teams where Hexerei Resonance is active, or N1C/N1W in teams where it is not.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Lost Prayer to the Sacred Winds: Assumes 12% DMG averaged.
Kagura's Verity: Assumes 2 stacks on the weapon passive.
Tulaytullah's Remembrance: Averages 25% Normal Attack DMG.
Surf's Up: Assumes dynamic stacking.
The Widsith: At [R5], this weapon performs closer to Tulaytullah's Remembrance. Klee cannot reliably use the Elemental Mastery buff, should she acquire it.
Solar Pearl: At [R5], this weapon performs slightly worse than The Widsith [R5].
Flowing Purity: Calculated with Bond of Life cleared. If paired with Fragment of Harmonic Whimsy (4) at max stacks, this weapon can perform as well as Tulaytullah's Remembrance.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Lavawalker: Klee's kit makes it harder to utilize Vaporize and Melt the same way another Pyro carry would, but her constant Pyro application makes this set viable.
Echoes of an Offering: With a 50.2% proc chance on average that gives Flat DMG to her Normal Attacks, this set is competitive with Lavawalker (4) and easier to farm.
Desert Pavilion Chronicle: Klee naturally uses a Charged Attack early in the rotation, making this set viable.
Fragment of Harmonic Whimsy: This becomes Klee's second-best set when you are specifically using Flowing Purity. For this reason, it's not recommended to farm this set specifically for Klee.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

builds.weapons:

builds.artifacts:

2. Ocean-Hued Clam (4)*
3. Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4)
4. Song of Days Past (4)
5. Maiden Beloved (4)

builds.main_stats:
Sands - Energy Recharge / HP%
Goblet - HP%
Circlet - Healing Bonus / HP%
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. HP%
3. ATK%
4. Flat HP
builds.tips:
Support Sangonomiya Kokomi focuses on just using her Burst before her Skill's duration ends to refresh her Skill duration. This allows 100% uptime on Hydro application from her Skill for Freeze / Electro-Charged teams.

Sangonomiya Kokomi's Burst dynamically alters her Skill's damage, this means that you cannot use her Burst to snapshot the buff onto the Skill and swap out.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Hakushin Ring: This weapon can provide Elemental DMG% for the team in Electro-Charged teams and will outperform Prototype Amber if you value damage more than healing.
Sacrificial Fragments: Can be useful as Sangonomiya Kokomi won't need to use her Burst to reset her Skill duration. This allows for smoother rotations as she will not face the problem of missing out on the reset for her Skill due to not casting Burst in time.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: With proper extension of Sangonomiya Kokomi's Skill, she can maintain near 100% uptime on this artifact set's 4 piece effect.
Ocean-Hued Clam: This ranking assumes Sangonomiya Kokomi is being used only for her Skill. If she is being used to attack onfield during Burst, this set may overtake Tenacity of the Millelith (4).

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: Prioritise first to ensure you can always use Burst before the Skill expires in order to maintain Hydro application.

builds.tips:
DPS Sangonomiya Kokomi aims to maximise her onfield damage during her Burst's duration. Outside of her Burst, her damage is lackluster and not worth building for.

Due to Sangonomiya Kokomi's high stamina cost on her Charged Attack, her best attack strings are:

At [C0]: N2D

At [C1]: N3D, which might result in a loss of Beidou Stormbreaker hits (DPS loss), and as such is only recommended in a Vaporize comp where the added Hydro application is favoured.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Hakushin Ring: This weapon can provide Elemental DMG% for the team in Electro-Charged teams, and is the best choice in those situations.
Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers: Although this weapon doesn't provide as much personal damage for Sangonomiya Kokomi, the 48% ATK% buff is usually more worth in most cases. This can be her best weapon in for overall team DPS if you don't already have another Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers user in your team.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: Although this set offers the lowest personal damage for Sangonomiya Kokomi, the 20% ATK% team buff can sometimes be better for overall team DPS.

Goblet - Elemental Mastery
Circlet - Elemental Mastery

1. Energy Recharge*
2. Elemental Mastery
3. Crit Rate / DMG
builds.tips:
Lauma offers access to Lunar-Bloom, as well as decreasing enemy Hydro and Dendro resistances. Because of her fluctuating energy requirements, she can offer different playstyles.

Typically features Lauma being the solo Dendro or being off-field for the majority of the team's rotation.

Lauma's Burst will typically buff 36 instances of Bloom-related damage, this is per target so in AoE it will deplete a lot faster.

Lauma's hold Skill will consume Verdant Dew stacks (obtained by triggering Lunar-Bloom reaction every 2.5s) - It is recommended to have 2-3 stacks before casting hold Skill, else tap Skill to conserve Verdant Dew stacks

Regarding Weapon Choices:
A Thousand Floating Dreams: Calculated with the passive giving 32 Elemental Mastery and 20% Dendro DMG Bonus
Etherlight Spindlelute: Assumes passive is always active.
Sunny Morning Sleep-In: This weapon assumes you are gaining the Elemental Mastery from the Skill portion of the passive.
Blackmarrow Lantern: This weapon assume you are using hold Skill for damage, if you are not, this weapon should not be considered

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Deepwood Memories: This set should be used if no one else on the team can use it
Night of the Sky's Unveiling: This set should only be considered if Lauma is on-field and teammates can hold both Deepwood Memories (4) and Silken Moon's Serenade (4)

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

builds.tips:
Layla's shield is very durable thanks to a good Base DMG Absorption and the number of Shield Strength modifiers she has access to. Furthermore, her personal damage scales well with Constellations (particularly 5 and 6). This build is a higher investment option than a pure Support build, choosing to sacrifice some shield durability in exchange for greater personal damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Harbinger of Dawn: Weapon ranking is assuming Layla is above 90% HP. As Layla is primarily used as an off-field character, she can usually have good uptime on the Harbinger of Dawn passive. Though it's possible that good uptime may be unrealistic depending on your team, or if you're facing enemies where corrosion is present.
Wolf-Fang: At [R5] this weapon performs similarly to Harbinger of Dawn [R5].
Favonius Sword: This weapon performs significantly worse than other listed options in terms of personal damage, but the extra particles for your team could be valuable.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set is by far the best option for Layla's shield durability, and very often the best set for team damage.
Blizzard Strayer: This is Layla's best set for personal damage in teams where enemies are affected by Cryo or Frozen.

builds.tips:
The goal of this build is to prioritize Layla's shield without building for personal damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Primordial Jade Cutter: This weapon sees more value on Layla when you're also investing into her damage to some extent.
Favonius Sword: favo.
Freedom-Sworn: This weapon can be a good support option in teams where Layla can consistently trigger the passive.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set is by far the best option for Layla, it complements her kit perfectly by providing HP% along with a teamwide ATK% and Shield Strength buff.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

notes:
Layla's shield is very durable thanks to a good Base DMG Absorption and the number of Shield Strength modifiers she has access to. Furthermore, her personal damage scales well with Constellations (particularly 5 and 6). This build is a higher investment option than a pure Support build, choosing to sacrifice some shield durability in exchange for greater personal damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Harbinger of Dawn: Weapon ranking is assuming Layla is above 90% HP. As Layla is primarily used as an off-field character, she can usually have good uptime on the Harbinger of Dawn passive. Though it's possible that good uptime may be unrealistic depending on your team, or if you're facing enemies where corrosion is present.
Wolf-Fang: At [R5] this weapon performs similarly to Harbinger of Dawn [R5].
Favonius Sword: This weapon performs significantly worse than other listed options in terms of personal damage, but the extra particles for your team could be valuable.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set is by far the best option for Layla's shield durability, and very often the best set for team damage.
Blizzard Strayer: This is Layla's best set for personal damage in teams where enemies are affected by Cryo or Frozen.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

builds.tips:
Linnea is a potent support for Geo units, providing Off-Field Damage with her companion Lumi, Lunar-Crystallize DMG when Moondrifts are present, and also providing Geo Resistance shredding and teamwide healing. In addition, when Ascendant Gleam is active, Lumi can also boost the team's Elemental Mastery, and further amplifies the Geo Resistance shredding.

Linnea can feed Lumi into her Ultimate Power form to execute the Million Ton Crush, a huge Lunar-Crystallize DMG nuke.

After inputting [1E], Lumi will continuously deal Lunar-Crystallize and Geo DMG. Inputting [4E] will lead to Lumi dealing all of her Lunar-Crystallize DMG via the Million Ton Crush, after which she will only deal continuous Geo DMG.

Both modes yield pretty similar damage, however using [1E] is generally more advisable in most teams.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Elegy for the End: This weapon is not worth considering outside of Zibai teams.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: Only necessary if you need to use Linnea's Burst to the heal the team.

builds.tips:
This build makes use of her entire kit, from her Elemental Mastery ascension stat, high rate of Electro application, and supportive driving capabilities. Making her an on-field driver for Quicken teams also minimizes her Energy Recharge requirements and is therefore her best use case relative to her other on-field teams.

Learning Blocks for On-field Lisa rotations:

Aim to use Lisa's hE and Q every rotation, and consistently weave together NAs with her tEs or CAs.

As an example: tE Q tE C N1 tE C tE …(Swap to teammates)… tE h3E 2[N2C]

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Vivid Notions: This weapon works best in teams where Lisa is enabled to plunge.
The Widsith: At high refinement [R4-R5] this weapon starts performing similarly to Lost Prayer to the Sacred Winds.
Flowing Purity: This weapon is more potent if you can reliably clear the Bond of Life. If you can, this weapon is equal to or better than Mappa Mare. At high refinement [R5] with its passive met this is on par with Solar Pearl.
Mappa Mare: At high refinement [R5] this weapon is equal to or better than Solar Pearl.

builds.tips:
This build makes Lisa a flexible buffing unit with off-field damage. She can play in a variety of teams but is most prominent in Quicken teams, other select teams can use her to fully buff an on-field character entirely.

Casting Lisa's Hold Skill does not require 4 entire seconds of charging to connect, as long as the indicator aligns with the ring, she can release for full damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
A Thousand Floating Dreams: Only used in Quicken teams.
Flowing Purity: This weapon is more potent if you can reliably clear the Bond of Life.
Hakushin Ring: This weapon can give a teamwide DMG% buff, often utilized in Quicken teams. Note that the passive cannot be triggered when off-field.
Mappa Mare: Only used in Quicken teams.
Favonius Codex: This weapon can be used in teams where Lisa's Energy Recharge requirement is high, or if your team values the additional particles.
Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers: This weapon sacrifices a lot of Lisa's personal damage for buffing potential on a single character. It can be recommended if Lisa is low investment and focused on buffing.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: Although Lisa is unable to fully trigger this set's buff, its teamwide DMG% buff can be of great value if no one else is equipping this set, like in a Quicken team.
Noblesse Oblige: This set's teamwide ATK% buff can be more preferable than to maximize Lisa's personal damage, though less common.
Gilded Dreams: Only used in Quicken teams

builds.tips:
This build aims to be the consistent trigger for reactions, most notably Hyperbloom or Overload. These reactions are primarily triggered by her Burst, which is why it's important to make sure its uptime is as high as possible. Avoid having a second Electro in the Overload team for this build since this could mess up the Pyro aura Lisa wants for this playstyle.

Lisa's Burst will not have full uptime when off-field due to its cooldown, sometimes on-fielding Lisa is advised to fill in downtime and trigger relevant reactions.

Big thanks to ArchedNosi for the help in putting together this section

Regarding Weapon Choices:

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Flower of Paradise Lost: This set only performs around 4-6% (varies according to the elemental composition of your team) better in terms of Hyperbloom damage. Therefore it's worth considering farming Gilded Dreams (4) instead since it's in a more resin efficient domain alongside Deepwood Memories (4).

Lohen is a Cryo DPS who deals the majority of his damage through Normal Attacks and Charged Attacks, but weaves Skill and Burst damage throughout. Lohen's damage is further increased when Off-Field DPS units deal large amounts of damage.

General Combo: N1 > CA > N1 > CA > E > N1 > CA > N1 > CA > repeat x3

Since using Lohen's CA will automatically use his normal attack beforehand, you do not have to worry about using multiple inputs for N1 > CA

Rankings assume Lohen is not run in a team where he himself triggers Melt reactions.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Deathmatch: At [R5], this weapon ranks similarly to Calamity Queller.
Vortex Vanquisher: If Lohen is consistently shielded, this weapon ranks similarly to Staff of the Scarlet Sands.
Sacrificer's Staff: At [R5], this weapon ranks similarly to Deathmatch.
Favonius Lance: This weapon's performance heavily depends on how much value the rest of the team gains from having lower Energy Recharge requirements.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Blizzard Strayer: This set can rival or outdo A Day Carved From Rising Winds if Lohen is fighting enemies that can be consistently frozen.  Do not use this set in teams that do not have consistent Cryo aura on enemies.
Marechaussee Hunter: This set is roughly equal to A Day Carved From Rising Winds if Lohen is used with Furina.

builds.tips:
Lynette serves as a Burst-oriented Anemo unit, that unlike other Anemo units does not excel in any one category such as personal damage, crowd control and buffs. Her potential for crowd control is partially dependent on unlocking her [C1] and [C4], and neither her ATK buff nor her personal damage reach notably high levels. What truly distinguishes her among Anemo characters is her unique capability to utilize Ousia-aligned attacks as a Fontaine character. This distinct trait enables her to neutralize specific enemy mechanics by triggering annihilation reactions, as part of the overall Arkhe mechanic.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sacrificial Sword: Ideally you would want higher refinement levels as in [R3]+ so its cooldown aligns better with your rotation.
Freedom-Sworn: While this is her best buffing weapon it would be extremely difficult to meet her Energy Recharge requirements without an Energy Recharge weapon especially while primarily using her as an off-field unit.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Emblem of Severed Fate: This set will give Lynette her best personal damage output, assuming the team is already running Viridescent Venerer (4).
Noblesse Oblige: This is a good option for her in teams that either won't benefit significantly from Viridescent Venerer (4) shred, or where putting it on her is the lowest total team damage loss.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
Crit Rate: Only assumes higher priority than Crit DMG when using Favonius Sword.

builds.tips:
On-Field Mavuika focuses on dealing large amounts of Pyro damage while riding her motorcycle: Flamestrider.

She's best played in a team with Cryo characters where she can reliably get up close with Flamestrider and trigger Melt, or she can trigger Vaporize specifically with Furina, Yelan or Mona.

Mavuika relies on a character exclusive mechanic known as Fighting Spirit. Due to these specific mechanics, Mavuika is also best paired with another Natlan character, with the ideal options being Citlali and Xilonen.

Mavuika can also take a more supportive role where she uses less field time and instead helps other DPS with her off-field damage and Pyro application, plus A4 passive giving the onfield unit a potent dmg% buff. Due to her passives, she would still like to Burst in teams where she can reliably gain a large amount of Fighting Spirit both for her damage and buffing capability.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Verdict: Ranking assumes 1 stack.
Beacon of the Reed Sea: Ranking assumes Mavuika does not take damage.
The Unforged: Ranking assumes dynamic stacks, shielded.
Serpent Spine: This weapon assumes 3 stacks. At [R5], this weapon will perform close to Beacon of the Reed Sea in Melt teams, and close to Verdict in Vaporize teams. At max stacks at [R5], it performs better than Redhorn Stonethresher. It should be noted that since Mavuika inherently has many sources of Crit Rate buffs, it is easy to overcap with Serpent Spine.
Tidal Shadow: Ranking assumes Mavuika gets healing.
Rainslasher: This weapon performs as well as Serpent Spine [R1] in a Vaporize team.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: If no other member of the team can hold it, this becomes Mavuika's best artifact set for Off-Field.
Marechaussee Hunter: Only to be used when paired with Furina - Performs mildly worse than Obsidian Codex (4).
Deepwood Memories: This set is a support option in teams with a relevant amount of Dendro damage (i.e. Kinich).

notes:
On-Field Mavuika focuses on dealing large amounts of Pyro damage while riding her motorcycle: Flamestrider.

She's best played in a team with Cryo characters where she can reliably get up close with Flamestrider and trigger Melt, or she can trigger Vaporize specifically with Furina, Yelan or Mona.

Mavuika relies on a character exclusive mechanic known as Fighting Spirit. Due to these specific mechanics, Mavuika is also best paired with another Natlan character, with the ideal options being Citlali and Xilonen.

Mavuika can also take a more supportive role where she uses less field time and instead helps other DPS with her off-field damage and Pyro application, plus A4 passive giving the onfield unit a potent dmg% buff. Due to her passives, she would still like to Burst in teams where she can reliably gain a large amount of Fighting Spirit both for her damage and buffing capability.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Verdict: Ranking assumes 1 stack.
Beacon of the Reed Sea: Ranking assumes Mavuika does not take damage.
The Unforged: Ranking assumes dynamic stacks, shielded.
Serpent Spine: This weapon assumes 3 stacks. At [R5], this weapon will perform close to Beacon of the Reed Sea in Melt teams, and close to Verdict in Vaporize teams. At max stacks at [R5], it performs better than Redhorn Stonethresher. It should be noted that since Mavuika inherently has many sources of Crit Rate buffs, it is easy to overcap with Serpent Spine.
Tidal Shadow: Ranking assumes Mavuika gets healing.
Rainslasher: This weapon performs as well as Serpent Spine [R1] in a Vaporize team.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: If no other member of the team can hold it, this becomes Mavuika's best artifact set for Off-Field.
Marechaussee Hunter: Only to be used when paired with Furina - Performs mildly worse than Obsidian Codex (4).
Deepwood Memories: This set is a support option in teams with a relevant amount of Dendro damage (i.e. Kinich).

builds.tips:
Mika is a Physical damage support who offers Physical DMG% through Detector stacks, and Physical Crit DMG at C6. He is additionally able to heal and provide some Attack Speed.

Do note that Mika's buffs apply to your on-field character and do not linger, this is something to keep in mind especially when playing him with Eula, as you will lose his buffs if you detonate her Lightfall Sword early.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Symphonist of Scents: Keep in mind that without his C4, it can be very difficult to meet his Energy Recharge needs to Burst every rotation and make full use of this weapon.
Favonius Lance: This is often Mika's best in slot option, as both an Energy Recharge weapon and one with a useful passive which generates particles on Crit. Weapons which provide Energy Recharge are prioritized to ensure good Burst uptime.
Engulfing Lightning: Weapons which provide Energy Recharge are prioritized to ensure good Burst uptime.
Prototype Starglitter: Weapons which provide Energy Recharge are prioritized to ensure good Burst uptime.
"The Catch": Weapons which provide Energy Recharge are prioritized to ensure good Burst uptime.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Ocean-Hued Clam: A good option if another party member is using Noblesse Oblige (4).
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: Not very helpful for teams that focus on Physical DMG.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - Energy Recharge / ATK%
Goblet - Hydro DMG / ATK%
Circlet - Crit Rate
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge
2. Crit Rate
3. ATK%
builds.tips:
Mona can use her Burst to apply the Omen debuff on enemies and allow teammates to deal more damage. This is most commonly seen in Freeze teams. Mona's main role on a Freeze team is to provide Omen debuff from her Burst as it is extendable via Freeze mechanics.

Note that you must apply Cryo before casting Mona's Burst for the extension to happen. Omen Freeze extension can be done by reapplying Cryo and Hydro aura consistently (this can be done with Cryo / Hydro infused Venti Burst + on-field Cryo / Hydro aura).

Mona's Burst applies bubble and Omen debuff upon cast, the count down timer only begins when the bubble is broken.

Frozen enemies can have their bubbles preserved as long as enemies stay in a frozen state. This technique makes Mona a priority Support for Cryo DPS teams.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers: Generally the best option on Support Mona because she can furthermore buff the ATK of your Main DPS. This however comes with an assumption that with this weapon, you still have 100% Burst uptime. If you do not, pick the other Energy Recharge weapon options for more Burst uptime.
A Thousand Floating Dreams: If the team does not need Elemental Mastery this weapon should not be used on Mona. If the team does need Elemental Mastery and does not benefit heavily from ATK%, this weapon can compete with Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers.

builds.tips:
Mualani is able to consistently Vaporize her Sharky Bites as colliding with enemies while on Sharky Surfboard does not apply any Hydro.

 If Mualani attacks a marked enemy with a Sharky Bite, any other marked enemies are hit with additional Missiles. Both the Sharky Bites and the additional Missiles are considered Normal Attack Damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sacrificial Jade: When paired with Obsidian Codex or Marechaussee Hunter, Crit Rate substats are no longer desirable.
The Widsith: On average, this weapon at [R5] performs similarly to Ring of Yaxche [R5]. It's worth noting that due to buff downtime and the possibility of getting the ATK buff, this weapon is worse for consistent performance and can end up worse. However, if you are willing to reset for good The Widsith buffs (DMG% or EM), it can outperform.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Marechaussee Hunter: Only to be used when paired with Furina. Worse than Obsidian Codex (4).

builds.tips:
Nahida can provide both damage, Dendro application, and buffs to her teammates. As such her build is focused on both maximizing her buff and her damage simultaneously.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Wandering Evenstar: If teammates significantly benefit from the Flat ATK this weapon gives, it has the potential to rival A Thousand Floating Dreams.
The Widsith: This weapon tend to perform better if Nahida is played on-field.
Skyward Atlas: The fewer the reactions Nahida triggers, the more potent this option become.
Lost Prayer to the Sacred Winds: This weapon tend to perform better if Nahida is played on-field.
Memory of Dust: The fewer the reactions Nahida triggers, the more potent this option become.
Solar Pearl: This weapon tend to perform better if Nahida is played on-field.
Favonius Codex: This weapon has the potential to rival A Thousand Floating Dreams if Nahida can actively trigger the passive multiple times by attacking on-field. Favonius Codex also has the potential to be a good weapon on Nahida in teams where her Energy Recharge requirement is high, or if your team values the additional particles.
Hakushin Ring: This weapon should only be used if Nahida is used in Quicken or Hyperbloom teams.
Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers: This weapon sacrifices a lot of Nahida's personal damage for buffing potential. Generally not worth the trade, but can be usable in teams where Nahida's damage share is already low.
Prototype Amber: Only to be used if the team otherwise lacks survivability.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Deepwood Memories: This is her best set if no one else is wearing this set.
Tenacity of the Millelith: In teams where Nahida's damage share is relatively low and there is another holder of Deepwood Memories and/or no need for the set at all, this can be Nahida's best set.
Flower of Paradise Lost: This set is niche, but very powerful in Bloom teams where Nahida triggers the Bloom reaction.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK%
Goblet - Geo DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. Crit Rate / DMG
3. ATK%
builds.tips:
Navia is a DPS that doesn't demand much field time. While most of her DPS is frontloaded into her Skill, she should stay on-field to take advantage of her 4s Geo conversion window. Her Burst lets her generate Crystal Shrapnel stacks and is important to use every rotation.

Full Combo: E N3D N2 E N3D (N2)

The final N2 is omitted if units like Fischl or Xiangling are used during Bennett's Burst before Navia.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Serpent Spine: One of her best performing 4-star options, at [R5] max stacks it will perform as well as A Thousand Blazing Suns
Fang of the Mountain King: Assumed averaged 0.5 stack of Burst%, and 1 stack of Skill% for Navia's second Skill
Wolf's Gravestone: Assumed passive is not active
The Unforged: Assumed shielded, dynamically stacked
Tidal Shadow: Assumes Navia is able to be healed. Otherwise, it is not recommended to use this weapon.
Earth Shaker: Assumes there is a Pyro-related reaction before her field time, this is achievable by using Bennett's Skill before swapping to Navia, or using an off-field Pyro unit like Xiangling or Mavuika

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Marechaussee Hunter: Furina teams only, performs as well or better than Nighttime Whispers in the Echoing Woods

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

builds.tips:
Nefer is a Lunar-Bloom DPS that primarily deals damage through her enhanced Charged Attacks while in the Shadow Dance state. Nefer's enhanced Charged Attacks consume Verdant Dew, and summon Shades that deal Lunar-Bloom Damage as coordinated attacks. Nefer also converts Bloom Cores into Seeds of Deceit, which enhances her Skill and Burst depending on how many she has consumed. The conversion requires Moonsign: Ascendant Gleam to be active.

Seeds of Deceit do not deal damage, and they cannot enable Hyperbloom or Burgeon. They are strictly to enhance Nefer's Burst and add Lunar% buffs to her Skill.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sunny Morning Sleep-In: Ranking assumes the Skill Elemental Mastery buff and part of the Burst Elemental Mastery buff.
Dawning Frost: At [R5], this weapon will perform better than Sunny Morning Sleep-In.
The Widsith: This weapon loses a lot of value if Nefer does not aquire the Elemental Mastery buff. Assuming she does get it, at [R5], this weapon will perform better than Blackmarrow Lantern.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - Energy Recharge / ATK%
Goblet - ATK%
Circlet - ATK% / Crit Rate
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. ATK%
3. Crit Rate*
builds.tips:
Nicole is a potent Support that provides both a shield and a sizeable Flat ATK buff to the entire team. The party member on field also gets an additional ATK buff after 3 seconds or instantly if they are a Hexerei character.

Nicole needs 4000 Attack to reach her full Attack buff.

Nicole's Burst provides additional damage that counts as being dealt by the triggering character, not Nicole herself. This damage matches the elemental type of the triggering character but does not apply the element at all.

Some teams do not gain a lot - if anything at all - from Nicole's Burst, in which case building Energy Recharge is not required.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Memory of Dust: As Nicole snapshots her own Attack when pressing Skill, it is advised to attack a few times before using Skill to benefit from this weapon's passive.
Starcaller's Watch: While not contributing a lot to high Attack, this weapon provides a reliable Damage Bonus to your onfielder.
Hakushin Ring: In teams where this weapon's passive becomes applicable, it increases in value and appreciates refinements.
Favonius Codex: This weapon can have significantly more value if the team's Energy Recharge requirements are dire.
Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers: This weapon severely reduces the potency of Nicole's buff, but brings a hefty buff of its own to the onfielder. Depending on the team, this weapon can be one of Nicole's best choices.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: If opting to Burst every rotation, Energy Recharge should be prioirtized until the requirement is met.
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. ATK%
3. Energy Recharge
builds.tips:
Assumes buffs from Bennett and 40% Geo DMG bonus.

Ningguang's Skill has a 6 second internal cooldown on generating energy, so for the sake of maintaining Burst uptime, wait 6 seconds to cast again if you need energy. It also breaks when cast on top of another Geo Structure, you can intentionally use a structure to break your first screen.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Memory of Dust: This weapon performs slightly worse if Ningguang does not have a shielder or consistent Crystallize uptime.
The Widsith: This weapon performs better than Skyward Atlas at [R5].
Solar Pearl: This weapon performs better than Skyward Atlas at [R5].
Flowing Purity: This weapon performs worse than Dodoco Tales if you cannot clear the Bond of Life from its passive.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Nighttime Whispers in the Echoing Woods: This ranking assumes Ningguang consistently has a Crystallize shield.
Husk of Opulent Dreams: This set performs on par with Archaic Petra (2) / +18% ATK set (2). With its inclusion in the strongbox, it has become a strong generalist option.
Thundersoother: This set is one of Ningguang's best options in teams where Electro Auras remain on enemies for the entire rotation.
Lavawalker: This set is one of Ningguang's best options in teams where Pyro Auras remain on enemies for the entire rotation.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - DEF% / ATK%
Goblet - Geo DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / Crit DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. Energy Recharge
3. DEF%
builds.tips:
This build is only recommended for [C6] Noelle. Noelle DPS is best optimized at [C6] where converts an additional 50% DEF into [stat:atk|ATK. Her ability to sustain her team with shields, heals and AoE damage makes for a reliable DPS when your team is built around her.

Combos:
- 1 / 2 Targets: N4 D/J. This isn't always feasible due to cast time (as well as knockback issues).

- Multiple Targets: N3 D/J.

With Redhorn Stonethresher, using Charged Attack can become more effective. While spinning, opening and closing either the map, or Adventurer's Handbook once will allow you to bypass the 5 second restriction.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Serpent Spine: When using Husk of Opulent Dreams (4), Serpent Spine stacks are prioritized over Husk of Opulent Dreams stacks due to their conflicting nature in gaining stacks (On-Field vs Off-Field).
Skyward Pride: Useful if you are unable to get Energy Recharge from your substats. Does really well while passive is active, but falls of in sustained fights. Comfort option that can lose to a high refined Whiteblind with stacks.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Husk of Opulent Dreams: Noelle snapshots her DEF on Skill cast. To get the full 4 stacks' DEF% boost during your first Burst, you need to wait 12 seconds before swapping in. In abyss, time spent before the chamber starts still generates stacks.
Marechaussee Hunter: Optimal for personal damage by a few percentage points when paired with Furina.

Goblet - Electro DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. Crit Rate / DMG
3. ATK%
≈ Elemental Mastery
builds.tips:
Ororon focuses on dealing damage off-field with his Burst. His A1 can also allow him to deal off-field damage whenever an enemy is dealt Electro-Charged damage or when the active character deals Nightsoul-aligned damage, with a cooldown of 1.8 seconds. With his A4 talent, he's able to give up to 9 flat energy per rotation after using his Skill, whenever the active character uses a Normal Attack, Charged Attack, or Plunging Attack. Ororon will also gain this energy if he is off-field.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
The First Great Magic: Ranking assumes 2 stacks. At 1 stack, it performs similarly to Hunter's Path.
Chain Breaker: Ranking assumes 3 stacks.
Range Gauge: This weapon will perform significantly worse if you do not have a proper healer to maintain 3 stacks. At [R5], this weapon performs similarly to Thundering Pulse.
Song of Stillness: Ranking assumes Ororon is being played with a healer and that the passive is active. Overheal counts for the passive.
Elegy for the End: This weapon provide more team utility in exchange for Ororon's personal damage.
Favonius Warbow: This weapon provide more team utility in exchange for Ororon's personal damage.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: While Ororon does focus on being an off-field dps, he has fantastic synergy with Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4), which plays an important part of being able to buff his team.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: Prioritize first until you can Burst off cooldown

builds.weapons:

≈ Celestial Gift (4)*

builds.main_stats:
Sands - Energy Recharge / ATK%
Goblet - ATK%
Circlet - ATK% / Crit Rate
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. ATK%
3. Crit Rate*
builds.tips:
Prune is a support that relies on triggering the Swirl reaction to buff characters. Triggering Swirl will convert her Banehunter Oathhammer (Skill) into the element she Swirls, and as long as her A4 talent is unlocked, hitting opponents with a converted Banehunter Oathhammer or her Burst will activate the Tolling Rally effect, which will provide DMG buffs to the team based on Prune's ATK. In addition, when a Hexerei character under Tolling Rally triggers a reaction, Prune gains an ATK% boost, and if that reaction was Swirl, the triggering character also gains an ATK% boost.

Prune's [C1] let's her restore 2 Energy every 1.8 seconds whenever a converted Banehunter Oathhammer hits opponents. How much total energy she can restore depends how often Prune is able to trigger Swirl during the duration of her Burst. Prune's [C6] increases her Burst duration by 4 seconds.

Tolling Rally does not apply to Prune herself.

Tolling Rally caps out at 4,000 ATK, usually not feasible to get unless Prune is in a Hexerei team.

Talent levels are not a necessity as they do not increase Prune's buffing capabilities.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Codex: In teams where Energy isn't much of an issue for Prune or the team, it is better to use a weapon that provides more ATK.
Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers: While this weapon significantly decreases the value of Prune's buff due to its low Base Attack, it can provide a 48% ATK boost to the DPS, which can be valuable depending on the team she is in.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Viridescent Venerer: Interchangeable with Celestial Gift (4) depending on if there are members in the team that are already holding one or the other set.
Celestial Gift: Interchangeable with Viridescent Venerer (4) depending on if there are members in the team that are already holding one or the other set.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

builds.weapons:

2. Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4)*
3. Ocean-Hued Clam (4)*
≈ Song of Days Past (4)
4. +15% Healing Bonus set (2) / +18% ATK set (2) / +20% ER set (2) [Choose Two]
builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK% / Energy Recharge
Goblet - ATK%
Circlet - Healing Bonus / ATK%
builds.substats:
1. ATK%
2. Energy Recharge
3. Flat ATK
4. Crit Rate*
5. DEF%*
builds.tips:
Qiqi has extremely strong healing capabilities, but it comes with the drawback of 0 way to battery herself pre-[C1] and no other meaningful way to support her team through her kit alone. Her A4 allows her to apply her healing talisman with her Normal Attacks which has a 30-second cooldown, and scales with her Burst talent. Energy Recharge will greatly benefit Qiqi, however you should not need to Burst every rotation.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sacrificial Sword: This is Qiqi's best option for maintaining Skill uptime, assuming the Frost Herald is able to hit an enemy. Should the weapon proc, wait for the Frost Herald to disappear before using her Skill again.
Favonius Sword: This weapon can place higher if the team values the particles generated from it. Otherwise, it is preferred to use Sacrificial Sword or other available options.
Peak Patrol Song: Thanks to Qiqi's high base Flat DEF, you can get a meaningful amount of the buff from this weapon. While using this weapon, you must Normal Attack twice to activate the passive. DEF% stats can be considered if your team does not struggle with survivability.
Amenoma Kageuchi: This weapon will significantly improve Qiqi's healing capabilities, however, it takes lower priority over weapons that provide Energy Recharge.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: This option is recommended only when paired with Sacrificial Sword to be able to maintain uptime on the ATK% buff from the set.
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: This set is a great option if Qiqi is placed in a party with a character from Natlan. Do note that she may have a rough time triggering an elemental reaction if you are solely using her Skill.
Ocean-Hued Clam: This ranking assumes Qiqi does not get her Burst up often and is instead relying mainly on her Skill for healing. If she is in a team which funds her enough energy particles, or if she acts as the on-field DPS, this set can overtake Tenacity of the Millelith (4).

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: cr-favo.
DEF%: Only when using Peak Patrol Song

Amenoma Kageuchi: This weapon will significantly improve Qiqi's healing capabilities, however, it takes lower priority over weapons that provide Energy Recharge.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: This option is recommended only when paired with Sacrificial Sword to be able to maintain uptime on the ATK% buff from the set.
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: This set is a great option if Qiqi is placed in a party with a character from Natlan. Do note that she may have a rough time triggering an elemental reaction if you are solely using her Skill.
Ocean-Hued Clam: This ranking assumes Qiqi does not get her Burst up often and is instead relying mainly on her Skill for healing. If she is in a team which funds her enough energy particles, or if she acts as the on-field DPS, this set can overtake Tenacity of the Millelith (4).

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: cr-favo.
DEF%: Only when using Peak Patrol Song

builds.tips:
DPS Raiden centers around using her Burst for damage and to generate energy for your team, while keeping her Skill active. Due to her Resolve mechanic, on-field playstyle, and the amount of energy she generates, she synergizes best with high-energy-cost off-field characters.

Start your rotation with Raiden's Skill.

Burst combo :
- Q > 4N4D (Recommended for general use)
- Q > 3N3C + N1C (Recommended with Attack Speed)

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Staff of the Scarlet Sands: It is not recommended to build Elemental Mastery for this weapon as her other offensive stats still outclass it.
Deathmatch: Ranking assumes single target. At [R5], this weapon can perform better than Staff of Homa
Skyward Spine: If using the improved combo thanks to the ATK Speed this weapon provides, it pulls slightly ahead of "The Catch" [R5].
Wavebreaker's Fin: At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Deathmatch.
Lithic Spear: Ranking assumes 2 stacks. At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Deathmatch. With less than 2 stacks, this weapon falls off in value.
Favonius Lance: While very subpar for Raiden's personal damage, this weapon can outperform most 4-star options if it helps team members with high damage share (i.e. Xiangling).

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set is not good for Raiden's personal damage, but can outperform other options due to the buffs it provides to the team.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Elemental Mastery: Elemental Mastery is only desirable in Aggravate

builds.tips:
DPS Razor focuses on using his Burst and Skill to deal high Electro damage which having non-negligable Normal Attack damage from his Claymore attacks. He is not incredibly reliant on other Hexerei characters, but it does help his Energy Recharge requirements and slightly increases his damage. Razor's DPS is significanly less potent before [C6], and his Energy Recharge requirements are notably higher.

Razor tends to prefer his Tap Skill over his Hold Skill for its cooldown advantage.

Razor's Burst resets the cooldown of his Skill, as such tE > Q > tE is the standard opener for Razor's combos.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Talking Stick: Ranking assumes Razor is being used with Bennett. If Razor is not used with Bennett, this weapon is much worse.
Skyward Pride: Gains significant value in teams where Razor's Energy Recharge requirements are higher.
Tidal Shadow: If Razor cannot be healed, this weapon ranks lower than Earth Shaker.
Earth Shaker: Assumes a teammate can trigger a Pyro reaction, otherwise, use other weapons.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Marechaussee Hunter: If Razor is used with Furina, this artifact set is roughly equal to A Day Carved From Rising Winds.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

builds.tips:
This Build focuses on Rosaria's Buffing/Energy generation/Cryo application capabilities. Though it does not mean that you dont invest in her personal damage, only that it's a lower priority overall.

Rosaria A4 passive provides a potent Crit Rate buff to her teammates via her own Crit Rate after casting Burst. This heavily incentivizes building enough Energy Recharge to Burst consistently and getting as close to 100% Crit Rate as possible

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: The best option in situations where you havent met your teams Energy Recharge requirements. Outside of that scenario you would be better off using a Crit Rate weapon that benefits her A4 buff.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: Unconditional 12 DMG% to any element that she reacts with, Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (2) also help her Energy Recharge requirement when there is a Nightsoul Burst triggered in the party
Archaic Petra: Only used in teams where she can reliably pick up Crystalized crystals
Instructor: Can be the better option in teams that prefer the Elemental Mastery buff over ATK, such as Hu Tao VapeMelt, while sacrificing her Crit Rate buffing potential

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

builds.weapons:

2. Polar Star (5✩)*
≈ Elegy for the End (5✩)*

4. Aqua Simulacra (5✩)
≈ Hunter's Path (5✩)
5. Thundering Pulse (5✩)
6. Amos' Bow (5✩)
7. Fading Twilight (4✩)
≈ Mouun's Moon (4✩)*
≈ Alley Hunter (4✩)*
≈ The Viridescent Hunt (4✩)
≈ Scion of the Blazing Sun (4✩)

builds.main_stats:
Sands - Energy Recharge / ATK%
Goblet - Electro DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. Crit Rate / DMG
3. ATK%
builds.tips:
Kujou Sara is a quickswap support who is able to buff your active character via her Crowfeather mechanic while dealing a decent amount of personal damage. When Kujou Sara uses her Skill, her next Charged Shot will leave a Crowfeather at the target location which explodes after a short while, granting the active character in its AoE an attack buff based on Kujou Sara's Base ATK. Active characters within her Burst's AoE will also receive the same buff or have its duration refreshed.

If building Kujou Sara for personal damage, it is recommended to let her receive the buff provided by her Skill to enhance her Burst, while letting the next active character be buffed by her Burst.

At [C2], you can skip the Charged Shot as her Skill immediately drops a Crowfeather, and save it for later on if you wanna swap back to her to extend her buffs. The Crit DMG buff provided by Kujou Sara's [C6] cannot be snapshot and is not shown in the attribute page.

For her Skill to generate particles, you need to perform a Charged Shot to place a Crowfeather after casting her Skill. If the Crowfeather explosion hits an enemy, it generates 3 particles.

Crowfeathers dropped through Kujou Sara's [C2] do NOT generate particles.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Skyward Harp: This weapon combines the highest base attack for Kujou Sara's buff with good stats for her personal damage, making it the best overall pick.
Polar Star: This weapon trades some buff strength for more personal damage. Can pull ahead of Skyward Harp if a quick E CA N1 Q rotation is performed to maximize its passive.
Elegy for the End: While providing a strong buff for the team and a large amount of Energy Recharge, Kujou Sara can have issues with reliably activating the passive in Single Target scenarios against small or mobile enemies.
Mouun's Moon: At [R5], this weapon performs similar to or better than Thundering Pulse, depending on your team's total Burst cost.
Alley Hunter: Kujou Sara's quickswap playstyle lets her take advantage of this weapon's passive well. Performs similar to Amos' Bow at [R5].
Favonius Warbow: While being bad for both Kujou Sara's buff and personal damage, this weapon can be a good pick if the team struggles with high Energy Recharge requirements.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige: A good pick for Kujou Sara if there is no other possible holder on the team, or if it allows for another buffing set to be added to the team.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

Forest Regalia: When used in its niche with Aggravate teams it can outperform Katsuragikiri Nagamasa. Outside of that its just an Energy Recharge stat stick.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Viridescent Venerer: The bread and butter artifact set for Anemo units, this set provides the ability to shred the enemy's elemental resistance while also buffing Sayu's Swirl damage output.
Ocean-Hued Clam: This artifact set trades Sayu's utility in favor of stronger healing and some extra damage. It is generally not recommended to farm this set for Sayu specifically.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK% / Energy Recharge
Goblet - ATK%
Circlet - ATK%
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. ATK%
3. Crit Rate / DMG*
4. Flat ATK
builds.tips:
Shenhe is a Support for Cryo characters, capable of providing damage buffs from her Skill and dealing damage while providing Cryo and Physical resistance shred from her Burst. Her Skill gives a few stacks of additional flat damage bonus to all nearby party members (5 stacks on Tap and 7 stacks on Hold). This additional flat damage bonus only works on Cryo attacks, so characters who don't use Cryo attacks don't benefit from Shenhe's Skill, do note that Cryo infusion from Anemo characters does act as a Cryo attack, so it benefits from Shenhe's Skill. Another thing to note is that the additional flat damage bonus Shenhe provides uses Shenhe's current ATK, so it doesn't snapshot.

Shenhe's Tap Skill gives a teamwide 15% DMG boost to Skill and Burst DMG. This lasts 10 seconds.

Shenhe's Hold Skill gives a teamwide 15% DMG boost to Normal Attack, Charged Attack, and Plunge Attack DMG. This lasts 15 seconds.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Calamity Queller: The passive of this weapon works perfectly with Shenhe's Skill because it's dynamic.
Vortex Vanquisher: This weapon's passive can't be stacked off-field, but it's still an ATK stat stick which provides Shenhe with a lot of ATK.
Favonius Lance: favo. Do note that Cryo Resonance provides 15% Crit Rate on Cryo-imbued enemies, so the need for Crit Rate on Shenhe is lowered. With refinements especially, Favonius Lance can outperform 5-star options under some conditions.
Lithic Spear: Ranking assumes 2 stacks.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
Crit Rate / DMG: Crit Rate substats become more relevant when using Favonius Lance.

Goblet - Elemental Mastery

1. Elemental Mastery*
2. HP%

builds.tips:
This is a Dendro team that requires a reliable Electro trigger unit to work, for which Kuki Shinobu is one of the best Electro units to perform this role.

Aim for around 850-950+ Elemental Mastery. Depending on whether you use an Elemental Mastery weapon or not.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Key of Khaj-Nisut: This becomes a better choice than Iron Sting at [C4] when you can gain the 3rd passive stack without extending your rotation time or taking extra field time. It can also net more Elemental Mastery than Freedom-Sworn at 3 stacks, assuming you have actually built some HP% Substats on your Sands/Goblet/Circlet, but this is usually not enough to out-perform the overall gains from Xiphos' Moonlight/Freedom-Sworn's respective passives.
Toukabou Shigure: This weapon performs very similarly in a Hyperbloom focused build to Iron Sting since its passive does not affect Hyperbloom damage.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Elemental Mastery: Essentially your only priority

builds.tips:
Aggravate adds flat damage to the triggering Electro attack that scales off Elemental Mastery and levels, which essentially allows Kuki Shinobu's personal damage to become relevant while also filling the role of a healer in the team.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Freedom-Sworn: While not having the highest personal damage it makes up for it in overall team damage through its competitive buffs.
Primordial Jade Cutter: Has the highest personal damage of any weapon on Kuki Shinobu but little to no utility, which means it usually falls behind in overall team damage.
Key of Khaj-Nisut: On paper combines a mixture of high personal damage with team buffs to potentially be her best weapon if you manage to get it fully stacked. This is usually an unrealistic scenario though with it taking up to 2-3 rotations to fully stack or forcing you to stay on-field to let it stack which is usually a net DPS loss.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Thundersoother: It's potentially your BIS set if you can maintain a constant Electro aura or near that, to be this consistent usually takes up to 3 Electro units (or possibly 2 Electro units and an Anemo unit like Kaedehara Kazuha or Venti swirling Electro)

builds.tips:
Outside of reaction teams where she can be a reliable trigger unit, Kuki Shinobu's damage contribution is usually insignificant. Hence her best usage case in those scenarios is building purely for utility.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Sword: The passive lets her provide energy to the rest of the team. A Crit Rate circlet or substats should be considered to reliably trigger the passive.
Freedom-Sworn: In teams where Kuki Shinobu can reliably trigger reactions, the teamwide buff from this weapon can make it preferable over Favonius Sword.
Key of Khaj-Nisut: Highest possible healing in exchange for weaker utility. Worth considering in teams that can't reliably trigger reactions for Freedom-Sworn or don't need extra energy particles from Favonius Sword.
The Dockhand's Assistant: Similarly to Key of Khaj-Nisut, this weapon trades utility for stronger healing.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige: When using this set, make sure to build enough Energy Recharge to cast Burst off cooldown and keep good uptime on this set's passive.
Instructor: Can be a cheap alternative on teams where the extra ATK% buff from Tenacity of the Millelith (4) or Noblesse Oblige (4) is not as useful.
The Exile: Can be a cheap alternative on teams where the extra ATK% buff from Tenacity of the Millelith (4) or Noblesse Oblige (4) is not as useful.
Ocean-Hued Clam: Provides a decent amount of extra damage on a full healing build.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

Goblet - HP%

1. HP%

builds.tips:
Sigewinne focuses on teamwide healing with her Skill. She can create Sourcewater Droplets, and absorbing these Droplets will give her a Bond of Life. Clearing this Bond of Life will give her Energy for herself. With her A1 talent, upon Skill cast Sigewinne gains 10 stacks of Convalescence, which boosts Off-Field Skill Damage. The damage boost provided when a Convalescence stack is consumed cannot apply to Sigewinne's own damage.

She is best used in a Furina-centric team.

At [C1], Sigewinne instead gains 18 Convalescence stacks.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Warbow: This weapon will place higher if the team values and needs energy. With this weapon, you want to build a little bit of Crit Rate to help proc the passive. It is unnecessary to level this weapon if you are not planning to use Sigewinne's Burst.
Elegy for the End: While the Energy Recharge from this weapon is unnecessary for this build, the passive can make it an effective choice in some teams. It is also not needed to level this weapon if you are not going to use Sigewinne's Burst.
Sacrificial Bow: This weapon can allow Sigewinne to work in slightly shorter rotation lengths at [R5], and allow for more uptime on Convalescence stacks. Do note that using Skill twice in quick succession will not double the number of Convalescence stacks. Does not need to be levelled if ignoring Sigewinne's Elemental Burst.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: Can be used in teams with a relevant amount of ATK scaling damage, and will generally outperform 2pc mixed sets in such instances.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK%
Goblet - Cryo DMG / ATK%
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. ATK%
builds.tips:
Skirk is a Cryo DPS that specializes on dealing damage on field through Normal Attacks, while also being able to take the role of a Burst Nuke. Skirk's Ascension 4 Passive provides her with a big Base Damage Modifier, therefore it is important to run her with only Cryo and Hydro Teammates to maximize this passive.

Skirk uses "Serpent's Subtlety" instead of Energy. She does not benefit from Energy Recharge, and her "Elemental Energy" is always 0.

- First Rotation with big Burst ready: Q E N5 C 2N5 C N5

- Rotations without big Burst: E N5 Q 2N5 C N5

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Calamity of Eshu: Assumes Skirk is constantly shielded. Without a reliable shield, this weapon is not worth considering.
Finale of the Deep: This ranking assumes the Bond of Life being cleared. Without clearing it, this weapon performs slightly worse than The Black Sword.
Summit Shaper: The ranking for this weapon assumes no shield. With a reliable shield, this weapon performs similar to Uraku Misugiri.
The Black Sword: At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Finale of the Deep [R5].

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Blizzard Strayer: This set is only useful against fully freezable enemies, in which case it becomes Skirk's best in slot. However, this setup makes it easy to overcap on Crit Rate with certain weapons.
Marechaussee Hunter: This set should only be used with Furina on the team, in which case it performs the same as Finale of the Deep Galleries (4). If Skirk is [C1] or higher, this set consistently outperforms Finale of the Deep Galleries for the Normal Attack playstyle.

Goblet - Elemental Mastery
Circlet - Elemental Mastery
builds.substats:
1. Elemental Mastery
2. Energy Recharge
3. ATK%
4. Crit Rate / DMG
builds.tips:
Sucrose's Ascension 4 Talent boosts the entire team's Elemental Mastery by 20% of Sucrose's total Elemental Mastery, which is why you want to stack as much Elemental Mastery as possible in her artifacts. As a support, most of Sucrose's value lies on her Ascension 1 and 4 talents, hence leveling her other talents (Normal Attack, Skill, Burst) is not required.

When utilized as a Viridescent Venerer (4) artifact set holder, make sure you don't immediately swap away after casting Skill/Burst, you need to be on-field when the Swirl reaction happens for you to trigger the 4-set effect.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Thrilling Tales of Dragon Slayers: This weapon does not provide any Elemental Mastery, however it overtakes the other weapons on the list when it comes to buffing your team. It offers a 48% ATK boost to the character you swap to, which is equivalent to 1 main stat. This buffs your DPS more compared to Sacrificial Fragments, which gives ~44 EM, equivalent to only 2 substats.
Hakushin Ring: This weapon is a good option for teams where Sucrose can swirl Electro to trigger its damage-boosting passive. In Electro-Charged teams, she can buff Hydro DMG% as well.
Favonius Codex: This weapon can be useful if you value the extra energy generation from the passive. You may need to get a few Crit Rate substats to trigger the passive reliably.

builds.tips:
Tartaglia is an on-field Hydro DPS and enabler. He works particularly well in a Vaporize team with Xiangling, as this allows him to Vaporize his Burst while also enabling Vaporize consistently for Xiangling.

Optimal Skill duration for [C0]: 7s, 9s or 12s.

Optimal Skill duration for [C1]: 9s, 11s or 14s.

Melee Burst: Only to be used in situations where you need only 1 rotation to clear as the Energy Recharge requirement to maintain Burst uptime is quite high (170-190%), you'll always want to start your rotations with Ranged Burst since it refunds energy to maintain your rotation well.

Skill Combo:
- 2 NA + 1 CA (Optimal)
- 3 NA + 1 CA (Recommended)
- 5 NA + 1 CA (Recommended combo if using Rust or Thundering Pulse)

Le combo Optimal combo provides more damage but consumes more stamina. Recommended combo provides slightly less damage but is stamina neutral and what most weapons should use.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Polar Star: Dynamic Stacking, will be done alongside of stacking Nymph's Dream (4)
Hunter's Path: Makes Elemental Mastery highly valuable, especially in rotations like N2C or N1C.
The Viridescent Hunt: Offers a valuable passive that helps a lot in grouping enemies, which can offer a ton of damage from Riptide. At [R5] this weapon performs almost as well as The First Great Magic.
King's Squire: Have to change double Swirl setup to use a Charged Attack instead of a Skill
Scion of the Blazing Sun: Assumed first Charged Attack does not get the passive. At [R5] this weapon performs as well as The Viridescent Hunt [R1] - due to this reason, it is better to get The Viridescent Hunt from the Battle Pass.
Blackcliff Warbow: Assumed 1 stack. At [R5] this weapon perform as well as Amos' Bow.
Hamayumi: Assumed weapon passive is not doubled.
Mouun's Moon: Assumed 260 Burst Cost in total. At [R5] this weapon performs as well as Scion of the Blazing Sun.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
+18% ATK set: This set with +15% Hydro DMG Bonus set (2) is only about 1-2 substats behind Heart of Depth (4), you can opt out for this option instead if you already have decent pieces.
Shimenawa's Reminiscence: Due to this set requiring Tartaglia to have around 150% Energy Recharge to reliably Burst every rotation, it falls behind the other options. However, it outperforms Heart of Depth (4) if used in a single rotation setting (ignoring Energy Recharge).

notes:
Tartaglia is an on-field Hydro DPS and enabler. He works particularly well in a Vaporize team with Xiangling, as this allows him to Vaporize his Burst while also enabling Vaporize consistently for Xiangling.

Optimal Skill duration for [C0]: 7s, 9s or 12s.

Optimal Skill duration for [C1]: 9s, 11s or 14s.

Melee Burst: Only to be used in situations where you need only 1 rotation to clear as the Energy Recharge requirement to maintain Burst uptime is quite high (170-190%), you'll always want to start your rotations with Ranged Burst since it refunds energy to maintain your rotation well.

Skill Combo:
- 2 NA + 1 CA (Optimal)
- 3 NA + 1 CA (Recommended)
- 5 NA + 1 CA (Recommended combo if using Rust or Thundering Pulse)

Le combo Optimal combo provides more damage but consumes more stamina. Recommended combo provides slightly less damage but is stamina neutral and what most weapons should use.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Polar Star: Dynamic Stacking, will be done alongside of stacking Nymph's Dream (4)
Hunter's Path: Makes Elemental Mastery highly valuable, especially in rotations like N2C or N1C.
The Viridescent Hunt: Offers a valuable passive that helps a lot in grouping enemies, which can offer a ton of damage from Riptide. At [R5] this weapon performs almost as well as The First Great Magic.
King's Squire: Have to change double Swirl setup to use a Charged Attack instead of a Skill
Scion of the Blazing Sun: Assumed first Charged Attack does not get the passive. At [R5] this weapon performs as well as The Viridescent Hunt [R1] - due to this reason, it is better to get The Viridescent Hunt from the Battle Pass.
Blackcliff Warbow: Assumed 1 stack. At [R5] this weapon perform as well as Amos' Bow.
Hamayumi: Assumed weapon passive is not doubled.
Mouun's Moon: Assumed 260 Burst Cost in total. At [R5] this weapon performs as well as Scion of the Blazing Sun.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
+18% ATK set: This set with +15% Hydro DMG Bonus set (2) is only about 1-2 substats behind Heart of Depth (4), you can opt out for this option instead if you already have decent pieces.
Shimenawa's Reminiscence: Due to this set requiring Tartaglia to have around 150% Energy Recharge to reliably Burst every rotation, it falls behind the other options. However, it outperforms Heart of Depth (4) if used in a single rotation setting (ignoring Energy Recharge).

≈ ATK%

builds.tips:
Tighnari's standard build revolves around him doing his Skill, Three Clusterbloom Arrows, an Burst, and promptly swapping out to another character.

There are three recommended combos that Tighnari can perform:
1. E C Q 2C - This is the most mechanically difficult combo. However, this combo allows Tighnari to have both his Burst affected by A1 and funnel particles from his Skill to his next Burst.
2. E 3C Q - Allows Tighnari's Burst to benefit from A1. However, this combo doesn't allow Tighnari to funnel particles from his Skill into his next Burst.
3. E Q 3C - Simplest combo to use, and generally weakest combo though it allows prefunneling. His Burst does not benefit from A1 in this combo. It is recommended at [C4] due to the Elemental Mastery buff.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
The First Great Magic: Weapon ranking is assuming two Gimmick stacks. It performs a bit worse in solo Dendro, but still remains a good option.
Elegy for the End: If Tighnari does not need the Energy Recharge that Elegy for the End provides, this weapon will perform worse than Scion of the Blazing Sun for his personal damage.
Fading Twilight: If Tighnari does not need the Energy Recharge this weapon provides, it is similar to Prototype Crescent in terms of damage.
Scion of the Blazing Sun: At [R5] this weapon is slightly worse than Thundering Pulse.
Slingshot: Significantly worse when fighting from a long range.
Range Gauge: This weapon is strong if you can get three passive stacks. At [R5] with three passive stacks, this weapon performs similarly to Thundering Pulse.
Song of Stillness: Weapon ranking assumes passive is active. At [R5], it performs similarly to Range Gauge.
Prototype Crescent: At [R5], this weapon is better than Slingshot. Notably, this weapon is heavily reliant on enemies having weakspots.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Gilded Dreams: This set becomes slightly better than Wanderer's Troupe if Tighnari uses his Burst every time he uses his Skill.
Deepwood Memories: This is by far Tighnari's best set if no one else on the team is using it.

Anemo Traveler can require a significant amount of Energy Recharge.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sacrificial Sword: This weapon's ranking assumes you make use of the passive Skill reset, however doing so will require you to stay onfield for a much longer period of time which may not be ideal sometimes.
Amenoma Kageuchi: This weapon reduces your Energy Recharge requirements to depending on refinement. At [R5], this weapon will be better than Sacrificial Sword.
Favonius Sword: Although this weapon does not provide much damage, the Energy generated by the passive can sometimes be more beneficial to your team. Make sure to build some amount of Crit Rate to reliably trigger the passive.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate / DMG: Will have higher priority for a full Crit build.

The Geo Traveler is ideally played as a quick swap DPS, swapping them in for short amounts of time to drop their Skill or Burst. While dealing decent damage of their own, the Geo Traveler is also a great battery for Geo units.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Summit Shaper: Assumes the Geo Traveler is shielded. Without reliable shield uptime, this weapon performs worse than Wolf-Fang.
Wolf-Fang: At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Haran Geppaku Futsu.
Finale of the Deep: Assumes Bond of Life is being cleared.
Festering Desire: This weapon increases in value if the Geo Traveler has high Energy Recharge requirements.
Amenoma Kageuchi: This weapon increases in value if the Geo Traveler has high Energy Recharge requirements.
Skyward Blade: This weapon increases in value if the Geo Traveler has high Energy Recharge requirements.
Fleuve Cendre Ferryman: This weapon increases in value if the Geo Traveler has high Energy Recharge requirements.
Peak Patrol Song: While not that good for the Geo Traveler's personal damage, this weapon can be appealing due to the utility it provides to the team.
Freedom-Sworn: While not that good for the Geo Traveler's personal damage, this weapon can be appealing due to the utility it provides to the team.
Favonius Sword: While not that good for the Geo Traveler's personal damage, this weapon can be appealing due to the utility it provides to the team.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Nighttime Whispers in the Echoing Woods: Assumes being shielded by Crystallize shards.
Golden Troupe: Assumes Skills are performed within 2 seconds after taking the field.
Emblem of Severed Fate: This set becomes better than 2 piece combinations in teams with high Energy Recharge requirements.
Archaic Petra: Subpar for the Geo Traveler's damage, but provides a good buff to the team.
Noblesse Oblige: Subpar for the Geo Traveler's damage, but provides a good buff to the team.
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: Subpar for the Geo Traveler's damage, but provides a good buff to the team.

builds.tips:
Pyro Traveler takes on a supportive role with Pyro application and the ability to hold supportive sets such as Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4), as well as weapons that provide team utility.

Because Traveler is NOT from Natlan, they do not count toward the Nightsoul Burst cooldown reduction. They can, however, trigger effects that rely on the consumption of Nightsoul Point, which their Burst can help with since it restores some of their Nightsoul Point.

Traveler's personal damage is rather low, so their damage investment is not a major priority for this build.

Traveler's Skill has two variations. Their Tap Skill forms a ring around the active character and deals damage when close to an enemy. Their Hold Skill deals damage as a conjoined attack whenever the active character deals damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Freedom-Sworn: If Traveler's personal damage is not something you are looking for, it is not needed to level this weapon for them.
Key of Khaj-Nisut: If Traveler's personal damage is not something you are looking for, it is not needed to level this weapon for them.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
DEF%: Only prioritized when using Peak Patrol Song.

Pyro Traveler's [C6] offers access to a Pyro infusion as well as a 40% Crit DMG buff to infused attacks. This allows them to be a potent DPS in a wide variety of teams. Ranking assume Overload, which is an easily accessible Pyro Traveler DPS team. However, potent Vaporize teams also exist.

N1C is Pyro Traveler's ideal combo

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Uraku Misugiri: If using a Geo teammate to trigger the passive, this weapon is roughly equal to Light of Foliar Incision.
Calamity of Eshu: Assume Pyro Traveler is shielded
Harbinger of Dawn: Assumes the passive is consistently active (always above 90% max HP). If this is inconsistent, it's recommended to choose other weapons.
Finale of the Deep: If the Bond of Life can be cleared reliably, this weapon is roughly equal to Summit Shaper.
Lion's Roar: At [R5] this weapon performs roughly equal to Harbinger of Dawn.

notes:
This section is dedicated to utilising the Anemo Traveler's Skill / Burst. Due to how little field time this playstyle takes, the Traveler can also fulfill the role of a quickswap style Sub-DPS.

Anemo Traveler can require a significant amount of Energy Recharge.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sacrificial Sword: This weapon's ranking assumes you make use of the passive Skill reset, however doing so will require you to stay onfield for a much longer period of time which may not be ideal sometimes.
Amenoma Kageuchi: This weapon reduces your Energy Recharge requirements to depending on refinement. At [R5], this weapon will be better than Sacrificial Sword.
Favonius Sword: Although this weapon does not provide much damage, the Energy generated by the passive can sometimes be more beneficial to your team. Make sure to build some amount of Crit Rate to reliably trigger the passive.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate / DMG: Will have higher priority for a full Crit build.
