builds.tips:
Donning her favorite mask, Varesa is a hypercarry Electro DPS who focuses on dealing damage through Plunge combos.

Generally combos for Varesa are very flexible, most of which consist of [EN1P EN1P (Q)] chunks that can be used at any time. Before [C2] you will not be able to fill your field time with just using all of your Skill due to her fast animations, this makes a secondary DPS a viable option, or can opt for doing CP CP to set up for your next rotation.

Short Hold Skill when starting the rotation with Obsidian Codex (4) will result in the highest DMG on average.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Tome of the Eternal Flow: Assume passive is not activated.
Surf's Up: Assume passive is not activated.
Cashflow Supervision: Assume passive is not activated.
Crane's Echoing Call: Assumes 3 Volcano Kablams instead of 2, no Energy Recharge requirements.
The Widsith: At [R5] this weapon performs on average slightly better than Tulaytullah's Remembrance.
Flowing Purity: This weapon assumed Bond of Life is cleared.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Obsidian Codex: It is entirely possible to become oversaturated on Crit Rate due to how much she gets via weapons and ascension. As such, if you find yourself overcapping frequently, consider farming Long Night's Oath (4).
Marechaussee Hunter: Only to be used when paired with Furina. Performs worse than Obsidian Codex (4).

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

builds.tips:
Varka is a dual element wielding DPS who adapts his weapon based on your team.

Varka always wants a second Hex character, a second Anemo character and two characters of the same PHEC type (Pyro/Hydro/Electro/Cryo) on the team. Not fulfilling these conditions will make his damage plummet.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Song of Broken Pines: This weapon's performance is highly reliant on using the attack speed buff. If you are having issues with combo execution in general, this weapon is not recommended.
Serpent Spine: At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Verdict.
Fang of the Mountain King: With this weapon, it is recommended to use Varka's special Skill instead of the special Charged Attack.
Tidal Shadow: Assumes Varka is healed to activate the passive. As this is not feasible in his current best teams, this weapon can quickly fall to the bottom of this list.
Talking Stick: Assumes the Pyro passive being active. At [R5], this weapon performs similarly to Fang of the Mountain King.
"Ultimate Overlord's Mega Magic Sword": While Energy Recharge is a useless stat for Varka, this weapon is one of the few accessible options that are actually reliable.

Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG / Elemental Mastery

4. Energy Recharge
builds.tips:
Venti is a potent Off-Field DPS who can buff teammates through Viridescent Venerer, or buff Anemo teammates through his Hexerei passive. With the addition of his Hexerei talents, he is no longer a Reaction-Based Off-Field DPS, although the playstyle is still utilized in some teams.

Make sure you don't immediately swap away after casting Burst; you need to be on-field for Viridescent Venerer (4) perk to proc on Swirl first.(E > Q to avoid this issue).

You can infuse elements onto Venti's Burst if you walk into it with an elemental aura on you. (e.g. Bennett Burst > Venti Burst > Walk up to infuse Pyro)

Check out [this link](http://tinyurl.com/ventiburstinfo) to see which characters can hit the enemies sucked and ragdolled in Venti's Burst.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
The First Great Magic: With three Gimmick stacks, this weapon performs better than Astral Vulture's Crimson Plumage.
Mouun's Moon: At [R5], this weapon ranks similarly to Hunter's Path.
Song of Stillness: ranked under the assumption that Venti is healed, thus activating its passive.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Viridescent Venerer: Prioritize this set unless someone else is holding it.

Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. ATK%
3. Flat ATK
4. Elemental Mastery
5. Energy Recharge
builds.tips:
Wanderer focuses on doing damage by activating his Skill and performing boosted Normal Attacks. It should be noted that in this state, he does not have knockback resistance so a shielder or other form of knockback immunity is recommended. It is not worth building Energy Recharge to Burst every rotation unless Wanderer is C2. Instead, bursting every other rotation is preferred.

External Buffs means the existence of a unit that provides ATK, or makes ATK less necessary (e.g. Bennett, Yun Jin)

- Desert Pavilion Chronicle and Signature Weapon Rotation: (Q) E CA (N3 repeat)

- Signature Weapon Rotation (non Desert Pavilion Chronicle): (Q) E (N3 repeat)

- Desert Pavilion Chronicle Rotation: (Q) E CA (N2C repeat)

- Other Artifact Sets Rotation: (Q) E (N2C repeat)

- Shimenawa's Reminiscence Rotation: E (N2C repeat) (Q)

- Swirl Driver Rotation: E (N3C repeat)

Regarding Weapon Choices:
The Widsith: Because Wanderer doesn't focus on Swirl damage, this weapon can be inconsistent if you get the Elemental Mastery buff.
Memory of Dust: This weapon can pull farther ahead than other options if Wanderer is shielded. If shielded, it maintains its ranking without Bennett, and performs better than The Widsith with Bennett.
Dodoco Tales: This weapon uses a different rotation of E N1 CAx6 N1 CAx6 to maximize its passive.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Shimenawa's Reminiscence: If Wanderer is [C2] or higher it is not recommended to run this set.
Echoes of an Offering: If your ping is above 100, it is not recommended to run this set specifically
Viridescent Venerer: In teams where the majority of the damage share is not linked to Wanderer, and there is no other viable holder of the set, he may use Viridescent Venerer (4).
Blizzard Strayer: This set synergizes well with the 20% Crit Rate that Wanderer gets from swirling Cryo. It is only recommended if you have close to 100% uptime on Freeze, which requires very specific teams. Despite this, with 100% Freeze uptime, this set is still worse than Desert Pavilion Chronicle (4).
Lavawalker: In teams with consistent Pyro application, this set is about equal to Echoes of an Offering (4).

builds.tips:
This build treats Xiangling as a reactionless off-field DPS used for her utility as well as her damage.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: The utility from a highly refined Favonius Lance can make it a weapon of choice in teams where Xiangling's Energy Recharge requirements are especially high.
Kitain Cross Spear: In teams where Xiangling uses her Skill twice per Burst, this weapon generates the most energy of any other weapon, making it ideal for longer rotations with high Energy Recharge requirements. It gains value in teams where Xiangling can trigger both Vaporize and Overload (i.e. Raiden National).

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Crimson Witch of Flames: This set can be competitive with Emblem of Severed Fate (4) on low Energy Recharge requirement teams where Xiangling triggers Overload in addition to Vaporize, such as Raiden National.
Deepwood Memories: This is a support option in teams with a relevant amount of Dendro damage, such as Kinich teams.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Elemental Mastery: In team comps where you can trigger both Vaporize and Overload at the same time (i.e. Raiden National), Elemental Mastery gets more value.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK% / Energy Recharge
Goblet - ATK%
Circlet - ATK%
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge
2. ATK%
3. Flat ATK
4. Crit Rate*
builds.tips:
Xianyun's main utilities are her jump height boost, healing, and Plunging Attack buff. While her healing is tied to her Burst Levels, the only way to increase her Plunging Attack buff is by increasing her total ATK.

Most teams will not use all three uses of Xianyun's Skill, since it is too time intensive to do so. A simple E Q is the ideal rotation for most teams.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Codex: With high team Energy Recharge Requirements, this weapon pulls ahead in value.
Wine and Song: Perform a dash to gain the ATK% from the passive.
Hakushin Ring: In teams with Electro units to benefit from the passive, this weapon ranks higher than Ballad of the Boundless Blue.
Prototype Amber: At low refinements, this weapon is not worth using. The lower Xianyun's total Energy Recharge requirements, the worse this weapon performs. At [R5] this weapon is similar or better than Ballad of the Boundless Blue.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Noblesse Oblige: Better than Viridescent Venerer if someone else already has the set or if Pyro/Hydro/Cryo/Electro damage is not a large source of the team's damage.
Song of Days Past: In Single Target scenarios, Song of Days Past (4) can be better than Ocean-Hued Clam (4). However, Ocean-Hued Clam (4) is more consistent.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK%
Goblet - Anemo DMG / ATK%
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. Crit Rate / DMG
3. ATK%
builds.tips:
Xiao is a Hypercarry that is centered around plunge attacks. Due to his expensive Burst, Xiao usually requires a high amount of Energy Recharge and/or at least one Anemo battery (Sucrose, [C6] Faruzan, Xianyun) on his team to help him recharge his Burst.

Xiao's Optimal Skill Rotation:  E E Q Plunge Combo

Xiao's Plunge Combo:

- Best combo for Xiao when fighting a single target:

 - NA CA hP (no collision possible)
 - NA > CA lP (collision only possible on Low Plunge)
 - hP spam (collision possible on High Plunge)

- Best combo for Xiao when fighting multiple enemies:

- hP spam

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Calamity Queller: In Teams with neither Bennett nor Xianyun, this weapon increases in value.
Crimson Moon's Semblance: While generally just a stat stick for the high base attack and Crit Rate substat, this weapon's passive can be utilized in teams with no or only Bennett's healing by performing a Charged Attack at the start of Xiao's Burst.
Vortex Vanquisher: Assumes no shield on the team. In teams with a shield or without Xianyun or Bennett, this weapon performs slightly better.
Skyward Spine: This weapon become significantly better choice in teams with very high Energy Recharge Requirements.
Blackcliff Pole: This Ranking assumes 0 stacks. In Teams with Bennett, this weapon performs similar to Calamity Queller. This weapon's passive is generally unreliable, but can lead to a slightly higher ranking when applicable.
Lithic Spear: This Ranking assumes 1 Stack. This weapon's performance varies greatly with refines and stacks. At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Blackcliff Pole with one stack and Calamity Queller with two stacks.
Missive Windspear: If Xiao can reliably trigger this weapon's passive, it performs closer to Blackcliff Pole.
Prospector's Drill: At [R5], this weapon performs similar to Skyward Spine given a reliable healer is present.
Favonius Lance: This weapon become significantly better choice in teams with very high Energy Recharge Requirements.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Marechaussee Hunter: This set pulls ahead of Vermillion Hereafter (4) with either Bennett or Xianyun on the Team. This set is hard to build around when using a Crit Rate Weapon, making other sets a better overall choice.
Long Night's Oath: While being a strong alternative to similar ranking sets, it is not recommended to farm this set currently as it combines an inefficient domain with a team- and skill- dependent damage performance.
Desert Pavilion Chronicle: Perform a Charged Attack at the start of Xiao's Burst to utilize this set fully. With a low base attack weapon and Bennett on the team, this set performs close to Vermillion Hereafter (4). It is not recommended to farm this Domain specifically for Xiao.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.

name: Xilonen

builds.weapons:
1. Peak Patrol Song (5✩)*
2. Freedom-Sworn (5✩)*
3. Favonius Sword (4✩)
4. Flute of Ezpitzal (4✩)*
5. Skyrider Sword (3✩)
builds.artifacts:
1. Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4)*
2. Archaic Petra (4)
3. Noblesse Oblige (4)
≈ Instructor (4)
builds.main_stats:
Sands - DEF% / Energy Recharge
Goblet - DEF%
Circlet - DEF% / Healing Bonus / Crit Rate
builds.substats:
1. Energy Recharge*
2. DEF%
3. Flat DEF
4. Crit Rate*
builds.tips:
Xilonen's main job is to decrease Elemental Resistances of opponents using her Samplers. This, paired alongside the ability to hold Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4), makes her a great asset to many teams.

Xilonen's Burst will heal the active character at intervals if 2 or more of her Samplers had their elemental types changed. Otherwise, she will deal additional instances of Geo DMG.

The resistance shredding from the Samplers occurs when Xilonen is paired with 2+ PHEC (Pyro/Hydro/Electro/Cryo) members. To activate them, press Skill, and continuously Normal Attack until her Nightsoul Points are full. This takes 2 or 3 Normal Attacks to do.

At [C2], Xilonen's Geo Samplers are always active, enabling her to be added in teams with 2 or more Geo members already present.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Peak Patrol Song: The buff from this weapon caps out once Xilonen reaches 3,200 DEF.
Freedom-Sworn: This option is competitive with Peak Patrol Song if the team can utilize both of its buffs. To proc it consistently, you either need to Burst every rotation, or add a delay between the initial Skill hit and normal attacking. It is not needed to level this weapon for Xilonen.
Flute of Ezpitzal: Refinements for this weapon are insignificant on Xilonen.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: If no other member of the team can hold it, Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4) should be the top priority for Xilonen. The other options are there in the event someone else has Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City occupied.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - Energy Recharge / ATK%
Goblet - Hydro DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / Crit DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. ATK%
3. Energy Recharge*
builds.tips:
Xingqiu's Off-Field DPS build centers on his ability to deal damage and apply an immense amount of Hydro off-field with his Burst. His Energy Recharge needs can change a lot based on team and constellations, and so do his list of recommended weapons. As such this serves a general idea of which weapons are most viable on Xingqiu.

Rotations:

*Quick*: E D Q N1

*Longer Rotation (C4+)*: Q N1 E D N2*

*One can use the Book trick method instead of the final N2 which involves pausing the game by opening a menu for a second to allow him to collect the particles.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sacrificial Sword: Despite this weapon providing the most energy for Xingqiu himself, it is a selfish weapon that extends rotation times which is a loss in team DPS. However, if you aren't worried about damage, its comfort is unparalleled as long as it is [R4+].

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Emblem of Severed Fate: This set falls off in terms of damage if Xingqiu has less than 130% Energy Recharge.
Noblesse Oblige: Offers party-wide Flat ATK boost, you can use this set on Xingqiu if nobody else on the party is able to use it better. If someone else on the team already has Noblesse Oblige (4), Noblesse Oblige (4) should not be used.
Blizzard Strayer: Outperforms Emblem of Severed Fate on Freeze Teams. However, it is generally not recommended to farm specifically for this set.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: Prioritize first if you can't Burst off cooldown

notes:
Xingqiu's Off-Field DPS build centers on his ability to deal damage and apply an immense amount of Hydro off-field with his Burst. His Energy Recharge needs can change a lot based on team and constellations, and so do his list of recommended weapons. As such this serves a general idea of which weapons are most viable on Xingqiu.

Rotations:

*Quick*: E D Q N1

*Longer Rotation (C4+)*: Q N1 E D N2*

*One can use the Book trick method instead of the final N2 which involves pausing the game by opening a menu for a second to allow him to collect the particles.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Sacrificial Sword: Despite this weapon providing the most energy for Xingqiu himself, it is a selfish weapon that extends rotation times which is a loss in team DPS. However, if you aren't worried about damage, its comfort is unparalleled as long as it is [R4+].

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Emblem of Severed Fate: This set falls off in terms of damage if Xingqiu has less than 130% Energy Recharge.
Noblesse Oblige: Offers party-wide Flat ATK boost, you can use this set on Xingqiu if nobody else on the party is able to use it better. If someone else on the team already has Noblesse Oblige (4), Noblesse Oblige (4) should not be used.
Blizzard Strayer: Outperforms Emblem of Severed Fate on Freeze Teams. However, it is generally not recommended to farm specifically for this set.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: Prioritize first if you can't Burst off cooldown

builds.tips:
This section is dedicated to using Xinyan as a shielder for her team.

Xinyan's Skill grants you a Pyro status upon contact with the shield. No matter how many times you switch characters, it will still apply Pyro every time you switch while shielded. This allows you to cleanse most elemental statuses that have been applied to you.

Shield uptime is also crucial to maximizing her Physical/Shield Support capabilities. If you have neither Sacrificial Greatsword nor at least [C2] (ideally [C4]) on Xinyan, you may want to build her as a DPS instead of Physical/Shield Support.

Generally not recommended for Pyro applications, as the pulses only happen at Shield Level 3 and are not very efficient at breaking Cryo shields.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Tenacity of the Millelith: If you can maximize the uptime of Xinyan's Level 3 shield, she can provide a constant 30% Shield Strength and 20% ATK% boost to the team, as long as the pulses hit an enemy. This means that Sacrificial Greatsword and Xinyan's [C2] are crucial to making this set shine on her. This set is not as recommended for her if you have neither of the two.

notes:

builds.tips:
Start your rotations with Yae's Skill to amplify her Burst damage.

Burst combo: E3 Q E3

After the first set of EEE you usually swap out and go through your buff rotation before swapping back to Burst

Take note that none of Yae Miko's abilities snapshot.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
A Thousand Floating Dreams: The team Elemental Mastery buff this weapon provides may make it desirable over other options.
The Widsith: At [R5] this weapon is equal to A Thousand Floating Dreams.
Solar Pearl: At [R5] this weapon is better than Tome of the Eternal Flow.
Hakushin Ring: This weapon's passive can buff your other team members, which may be more beneficial than Yae Miko's personal damage depending on the team and rotation used. Note that the passive cannot be triggered when off-field.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Emblem of Severed Fate: This set works best for Yae in multi-target scenarios where her Burst hits multiple enemies and she is bursting every rotation. Is it typically not recommended to build very high Energy Recharge as this comes at the expense of offensive stats, so should you be playing in teams without a good battery (such as Raiden or Fischl), it may be optimal to use Yae's Burst every other rotation or not at all.
Golden Troupe: Yae's best artifact set in single target. It is also the preferred choice if Yae does not Burst.
Thundersoother: Assuming you are able to maintain 100% uptime on its passive, this is competitive to be Yae's BiS set. This set works best for Yae Miko in Electro-Charged teams.
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set trades off a portion of Yae's damage in return for a party wide 20 ATK% buff. If you have good pieces as a result of farming Pale Flame (4) (for Eula or otherwise) this can be a good option for her. Not recommended to specifically farm this set for Yae.

builds.tips:
Calculations assume Bennett. Yanfei is a Charged Attack damage dealer that utilizes a stack mechanic to increase the damage of her Charged Attacks.

Yanfei typically does N2C as her combo to maximize her damage, especially during her Burst state. With two Hydro characters, this results in consistent Vaporize hits. However, if you only have one Hydro character on your Yanfei team, it may be best to do N3C instead.

Yanfei's A4 being reliant on a crit hit makes her ideal crit ratio skew toward 3:4 and 3:5 instead of the standard 1:2.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Reliquary of Truth: Assumes the weapon passive gives 80 Elemental Mastery.
Tome of the Eternal Flow: Calculated with reliable stacking, such as with Furina. Without reliable stacking, this weapon performs worse than Flowing Purity [R5].
Cashflow Supervision: Calculated with reliable stacking, such as with Furina. Without reliable stacking, this weapon performs worse than Flowing Purity [R5].
Surf's Up: Calculated with reliable stacking, such as with Furina. Without reliable stacking, this weapon performs worse than Flowing Purity [R5].
Starcaller's Watch: Ranks similarly to A Thousand Floating Dreams if Yanfei is [C4+].
Flowing Purity: Calculated with Bond of Life cleared. Loses significant value if run without a consistent healer.
The Widsith: At [R5], this weapon performs as well as Cashflow Supervision.
Dawning Frost: Calculated with the weapon passive always active. At [R5], this weapon performs as well as Tome of the Eternal Flow.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Marechaussee Hunter: Yanfei's BiS set if used with Furina.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: Some teams will prioritize Energy Recharge over Elemental Mastery if it means Yanfei is able to Burst every rotation. Otherwise, it is safe to ignore Energy Recharge.

Goblet - HP%

2. HP%

builds.tips:
Yaoyao focuses on healing and applying Dendro with her Skill and Burst. She can be used in a wide range of team archetypes, but do note her Dendro application from her Skill alone is generally insufficient for Bloom/Hyperbloom/Burgeon teams as a solo Dendro option.

The additional Yueguis summoned by the Burst are removed when Yaoyao leaves the field.

If using Favonius Lance, you can perform Skill followed by two Normal Attacks in order to trigger the passive.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: This weapon is a good generalist option for Yaoyao, providing Energy Recharge with a useful passive.
Rightful Reward: This weapon performs worse if you are not planning to use Yaoyao's Burst.
Kitain Cross Spear: This weapon can be better than Favonius Lance in teams where Yaoyao has a significant amount of Bloom ownership.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Deepwood Memories: This set is the default option in teams where you care about reducing Dendro resistance and nobody else on the team is using it.
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set is a good option in teams where reducing Dendro resistance is not important, such as Yaoyao, Anemo, two Electro damage dealers (Aggravate).
Instructor: Similarly to Tenacity, this set can be a good option in teams where reducing Dendro resistance is not important, or if someone else on the team is already using Deepwood Memories. Do note that Yaoyao has to trigger a reaction on-field to provide the 4pc bonus. Additionally, your stat potential on this set will be limited as it does not come in 5* rarity.
Ocean-Hued Clam: This set is a good option in teams where you on-field Yaoyao and a supportive set is not needed.
Flower of Paradise Lost: This option is specifically for Nilou Bloom teams where Yaoyao has a significant amount of Bloom ownership and is built with Elemental Mastery. Do note that Ocean-Hued Clam (4) can still be better in these teams, assuming you on-field Yaoyao.
Gilded Dreams: This option is specifically for Nilou Bloom teams where Yaoyao has a significant amount of Bloom ownership and is built with Elemental Mastery. Do note that Ocean-Hued Clam (4) can still be better in these teams, assuming you on-field Yaoyao.
+80 EM set: This option is specifically for Nilou Bloom teams where Yaoyao has a significant amount of Bloom ownership and is built with Elemental Mastery. Do note that Ocean-Hued Clam (4) can still be better in these teams, assuming you on-field Yaoyao.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: er-req.
Crit Rate: Try to build some Crit Rate through substats if using Favonius Lance. Crit Rate is not important on other weapons.

Yelan's primary role is as an off-field Hydro DPS who can also enable reactions thanks to her elemental application. Similarly to Xingqiu, her exquisite throws from Burst require your character to perform the Normal Attack action.

Big thanks to Vladone ツ#9281 for helping out

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Mouun's Moon: This weapon works best at [R3+] and makes HP% Goblet potentially a stronger option than Hydro DMG% in teams with significant DMG% buffing.
Hunter's Path: This weapon performs slightly better than Slingshot, however as Yelan does not care about Base ATK and the passives are less than ideal for her, it is not recommended to pull or prioritize using it on Yelan over Slingshot if you have characters who could benefit more from this weapon.
Polar Star: This weapon performs slightly better than Slingshot, however as Yelan does not care about Base ATK and the passives are less than ideal for her, it is not recommended to pull or prioritize using it on Yelan over Slingshot if you have characters who could benefit more from this weapon.
Thundering Pulse: This weapon performs slightly better than Slingshot, however as Yelan does not care about Base ATK and the passives are less than ideal for her, it is not recommended to pull or prioritize using it on Yelan over Slingshot if you have characters who could benefit more from this weapon.
Skyward Harp: This weapon performs slightly better than Slingshot, however as Yelan does not care about Base ATK and the passives are less than ideal for her, it is not recommended to pull or prioritize using it on Yelan over Slingshot if you have characters who could benefit more from this weapon.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Marechaussee Hunter: This set should only be used for on-field Yelan with Furina, but in such cases it is the strongest set option.

Elegy for the End: This weapon is not ideal for Yelan's personal damage so should only be used in teams which can utilise the buff well.
Aqua Simulacra: Still the best option for personal damage even at high Energy Recharge requirements, but may be unfeasible to run in some teams as the Energy Recharge requirement can become very difficult to reach without an Energy Recharge weapon.

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Marechaussee Hunter: This set should only be used for on-field Yelan with Furina, but in such cases it is the strongest set option.

builds.tips:
Yoimiya is an on-field Pyro DPS who specializes in dealing damage through her Normal Attacks. It is recommended to play Yoimiya with some source of resistance to interruption, as breaking her Normal Attack string is a significant damage loss.

This ranking assumes a Vaporize team with Bennett.

Yoimiya's hardest hitting part of her Normal Attack string is her N5, and in Vaporize teams you should be Vaporizing the N1, N3 and N5.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Polar Star: Assumes 2 stacks by performing a quick aimed shot before using Skill.
Amos' Bow: Weapon rankings are assuming 3 stacks. At 2 stacks it performs worse than Slingshot [R5]. The closer you are to the enemy, the less stacks this weapon gets.
Rust: Scales well with refinements, at [R5] this weapon performs similar to Polar Star. At [R4+] it outperforms Slingshot [R5].

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Gilded Dreams: This artifact set should only be used in teams where Yoimiya frequently triggers elemental reactions.
Echoes of an Offering: An okay choice if you have a good set already, however not recommended to farm as it is very inconsistent and becomes even less useful if your ping is >100 or generally jumping a lot.

2. Energy Recharge

builds.tips:
Yun Jin is a Support character for Normal Attack focused characters who can deal off-field damage via her Burst. Yun Jin's Burst grants nearby party members 30 stacks of additional flat damage bonus on Normal Attacks which scales with Yun Jin's current DEF.

This means that external DEF buffs like from Gorou's Skill and/or Burst doesn't provide much to Yun Jin since it only buffs active characters and not off-field ones.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: favo.
Kitain Cross Spear: Although the Elemental Mastery Main Stat isn't useful for Yun Jin the passive decreases her Energy Recharge requirements by a lot, especially at higher refinements. Note that she must cast Burst first before Skill, as the passive drains 3 energy initially when Skill is cast. This weapon can be left at level 1 while still getting the full effect from the passive.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Crit Rate: cr-favo.

name: Zhongli

builds.weapons:
1. Black Tassel (3✩)
≈ Favonius Lance (4✩)*
builds.artifacts:
1. Archaic Petra (4)*
≈ Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City (4)*
2. Deepwood Memories (4)*
≈ Instructor (4)*
3. Tenacity of the Millelith (4)*
4. +20% HP set (2) / +20% HP set (2)
builds.main_stats:
Sands - HP%
Goblet - HP%
Circlet - HP% / Crit Rate
builds.substats:
1. HP%
2. Flat HP
3. Energy Recharge
builds.tips:
This kind of playstyle is very straightforward; stack as much HP as possible. Prioritize HP scaling polearms such as Black Tassel and artifact sets such as Tenacity of the Millelith (4) to capitalize on Zhongli's Shield. Most of the time, especially on a HP-focused Zhongli, casting Burst is a DPS loss due to the cast time, which is why you'd only want to cast Burst for the Crowd Control it offers, and not for damage.

Note that if paired with Xingqiu and/or Beidou, the damage reduction from orbitals from Xingqiu's Skill and Burst and/or Beidou's Burst can be utilized on Zhongli's shield, making it basically unbreakable.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Favonius Lance: Use this weapon over Black Tassel if you value the team energy (Note that you need to build some amount of Crit Rate if you are running Favonius Lance to trigger the passive)

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Archaic Petra: Can be more useful than Tenacity of the Millelith (4) in teams that don't scale as well with ATK buffs or already have another Tenacity of the Millelith holder. Though it can be an awkward set to utilize well without extending rotation times, since the Holder of the set (Zhongli in this case) has to pickup the shard for the buff to take effect.
Scroll of the Hero of Cinder City: Preferred over Archaic Petra if there are multiple Crystallize-compatible Elements or Geo characters that want DMG% bonus.
Deepwood Memories: This set works best in Dendro reaction focused teams (Bloom/Burgeon/Hyperbloom/Spread) that prefer having Deepwood Memories on Zhongli so that they can run more damage focused sets such as Gilded Dreams / Flower of Paradise Lost instead for the highest overall damage gain. That said it means keeping the enemies near your pillars to have good uptime on the Dendro resistance shred effect, which can be difficult with no grouping abilities in a team.
Instructor: This set is usually only used in a few heavily reaction focused teams, such as Ganyu Melt or Hu Tao Vape, where you're maximising your damage in exchange for a weaker shield. Though Zhongli's shield is still usually more than strong enough even with fewer substat rolls to strengthen it.
Tenacity of the Millelith: This set is best in slot for Shield HP, but it's usually inconsistent against Bosses and needs good Construct placement, due to it's inconsistency, it's better to use other buffing sets that are mentioned if they work for the team.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - ATK% / HP%
Goblet - Geo DMG
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. ATK%
3. HP%
4. Energy Recharge
builds.tips:
Zhongli can be utilized as a Geo Burst damage dealer due to his short Burst cooldown (12s) and low energy cost (40 Energy). His Skill Hold Cast (Shield) scales with HP and at Ascension 4, you would unlock a talent that allows your Burst to scale with 33% of Zhongli's Max HP, making him a great source of Shield and damage. Though in certain teams without either very high investment into his damage or enough time in the rotation to burst without extending rotation times his burst can be a DPS loss.

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Lithic Spear: Assumes 3 stacks.
Blackcliff Pole: Assumes 0 stacks.
Favonius Lance: If the team values the particles from this weapon, then it can be better than all the other options mentioned for overall team damage (Note that you need to build some amount of Crit Rate if you are running Favonius Lance to trigger the passive)

Regarding Artifact Sets:
Archaic Petra: Combined with Noblesse Oblige (2), on lower Energy Recharge requirements where Zhongli doesn't need to worry about his energy needs, this set pulls ahead of Emblem of Severed Fate (4).
Noblesse Oblige: This set provides Zhongli more utility when casting his Burst, you can use this set on Zhongli if there is nobody else on the team who's equipping this set.
Tenacity of the Millelith: Just like Noblesse Oblige (4), this set provides Zhongli with more utility if his Skill hits an enemy. This set also provides Zhongli with stronger shields.

builds.main_stats:
Sands - DEF%
Goblet - DEF%
Circlet - Crit Rate / DMG
builds.substats:
1. Crit Rate / DMG
2. DEF%
3. Energy Recharge*
4. Elemental Mastery
builds.tips:
Zibai is an on-field damage dealer that focuses on getting enough stacks to use her enhanced Skill via using Normal Attacks and proccing Lunar-Crystallized reaction. This leads to Zibai wanting as much field time as possible to make use of all 4 of her Skill charges along with getting to the final hits of her NA chain.

Zibai requires another Moonsign character in her team to unlock a large portion of her damage potential.

- Full Combo: E 4[N4E] - Replace 1 N4E with N1QE if Burst is available

- E n4 n4 E n1E n1E QE - Condensed rotation, does not offer more damage and tends to put you in teammate's Skill cooldown time, only used when needing front-loaded damage

Combo for Zibai can be unreliable due to her teams generally having slower off - field Hydro appliers that interfere with her proccing Lunar-Crystallized at the right moment, which makes her combo more flexible to use depending on the situation

Regarding Weapon Choices:
Uraku Misugiri: Assumes passive effect is doubled.
Light of Foliar Incision: Assumes Zibai uses all 28 stacks.
Harbinger of Dawn: Ranking assumes the passive is active 90% of the time. Without consistent healing/shielding, this weapon performs worse than Flute of Ezpitzal [R5].
Splendor of Tranquil Waters: Ranking assumes no HP fluctuation.
Haran Geppaku Futsu: Assumes passive is at max stacks.

Regarding Artifact Substats Priority:
Energy Recharge: It is not realistic nor recommended to build enough ER% to Burst every rotation for Zibai. It is not recommended to build ER% at the expense of offensive stats. Can be good substat to reach energy threshold to Burst 2 out of every 3 rotation for more damage but can be ignored entirely
